Title: Data Analyst
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
KAYAK/momondo, part of Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG), is a leading travel search engine. With billions of queries across our platforms, we help people find their perfect flight, stay, rental car and vacation package. We’re also transforming business travel with a new corporate travel solution, KAYAK for Business.

As an employee of KAYAK, you will be part of a travel company that operates a portfolio of global metasearch brands including momondo, Cheapflights and HotelsCombined, among others. From start-up to industry leader, innovation is in our DNA and every employee has an opportunity to make their mark. Our focus is on building the best travel search engine to make it easier for everyone to experience the world.

KAYAK’s Organic Growth team, within Marketing SEO, uses travel search data to create timely, data-driven stories for media and public relations. We’re looking for a Data Analyst who can turn raw search signals into clear, reusable datasets, automated pipelines, and easy-to-use dashboards that support global PR and editorial campaigns.

This role will be required to work from our Copenhagen office at least 3 days per week.

In This Role, You Will


Receive and scope data requests from PR and local media, then deliver clean, press-ready datasets.
Work with PR and communications partners to shape data angles and check feasibility.
Build and maintain automated SQL/Python pipelines and templates for recurring requests.
Create and maintain self‑service dashboards and lightweight tools (e.g., Superset, Datawrapper, Tableau) so teams can quickly explore trends.
Validate data quality and collaborate with Data Engineering to improve underlying models and delivery.
Document methods and make outputs easy for non-technical audiences to use.


Please Apply If You Have


A Bachelor’s degree in Economics, Statistics, Data Science, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field.
2-4 years of experience in a data analyst, business analyst, marketing analyst, or similar role.
Practical experience working with SQL and extracting/manipulating large datasets.
Hands-on experience in Python (or similar) for data processing and automation.
Familiarity with visualization tools (Superset, Datawrapper, Tableau, or similar).
Strong analytical thinking and the ability to translate complex data into clear, media-ready insights.
Comfortable explaining technical decisions to non-technical partners and delivering work on tight timelines.


Nice-to-have


Experience building reusable pipelines or automation for recurring reports.
Experience working with marketing, search, or travel data.
Experience building lightweight self-serve dashboards.


Benefits And Perks


Work from (almost) anywhere for up to 20 days per year
Focus on mental health and well-being:
Company-paid therapy sessions through SpringHealth
Company-paid subscription to HeadSpace
Company-wide week off a year - the whole team fully recharges (and returns without a pile-up of work!)
No meeting Fridays
Paid parental leave
6 weeks paid vacation + a day off for your birthday
Paid volunteer time
Focus on your career growth:
Development Dollars
Leadership development
Access to thousands of on-demand e-learnings
Travel Discounts
Employee Resource Groups
Pension plan contributions
Private health & life insurance
Lunch provided five days per week & breakfast once a week
Professional espresso machine
Awesome office in the city centre of Copenhagen (Rådhuspladsen)
Monthly social events, yoga, Thursday bars

Inclusion

At KAYAK, we want everyone to have the space to grow, share ideas and do great work. That’s why we’re focused on hiring the best talent from all walks of life and experiences, supporting them well and making sure no one feels like they have to fit a mold to belong here.

If you need accommodations during the application or interview process, or on the job, we’re here to support you. Please reach out to your recruiter to request any accommodations.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Coalesce Management Consultancy (CMC) have a consulting assignment available for a Senior Data Analyst for Change Signal/Predictive Insights for a client of ours in the Wind Energy Sector.




CMC provide expert engineering solutions on an international capacity for Advanced Engineering projects. Established in 2016, we set out our mission to be the best service-driven consulting business in global STEM markets, and after rapid growth in just a few years, we’re already delivering true expertise to major technology brands and leading companies around the world.




As we expand our reach and take on new projects, we have assignments available for experienced professionals to join our consultancy and deliver the expert solutions and high level of service we’re known for across multiple ventures.




By partnering with CMC, you get the chance to work with some of the world’s leading technology specialists. From our global offices we support you with each new project, providing a complete scope of work definition ahead of any engagement, so you know exactly what’s required by the assignment, before you land on-site. As the project is completed, we can continue to work with you by setting up your next project from our global network of leading technology companies.




Join our latest project:




We are looking for someone with:




Strong experience analysing large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and forward-looking insights
Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable recommendations for engineering and business teams
Experience with predictive analytics or forecasting (identifying future changes, risks, or opportunities)
Background working in engineering, manufacturing, or industrial environments (wind experience highly desirable)
Proven ability to communicate insights to non-technical stakeholders and influence decision-making
Experience working closely with product or engineering teams to improve development processes through data
Senior-level experience with a hands-on, proactive approach and ability to work independently
Comfortable operating in ambiguous environments and helping shape new data-driven capabilities







If you have the relevant capability and are interested in pursuing this assignment, talk to our sales consultants today. finlay.gethin@cmcexpertisedelivered.com







Get in touch




Learn more about Coalesce Management Consulting, our projects and the support we provide to our consultants at https://expertisedelivered.com




No terminology in this advert is intended to discriminate on the grounds of age or experience, and we confirm that we are happy to accept applications from persons of any age or experience for this role.

Title: Data Analyst - Product
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Lunar


1260 København, Denmark
Full-time
Hybrid
No Salary Information Available


Posted 13 days ago

Apply for this job

Lunar is a dynamic company revolutionizing the banking industry with advanced technology. As a hybrid of tech and financial services, Lunar uses its own banking license to compete directly with traditional banks. The Data Analyst - Product role exists to support Lunar's mission of transforming digital banking across the Nordics, employing data-centric methods to refine product strategies.

In this role you can expect to have the responsibilities:


Translate complex data into actionable insights for product strategy decisions.
Track and define KPIs to evaluate product initiatives.
Develop and maintain dashboards and analytics tools for product metrics visibility.
Collaborate across teams including Product Managers and Engineers for data integration.
Enhance the data ecosystem with Data Engineering and Analytics teams.
Craft engaging data narratives through visualizations to influence stakeholders.


This Role Comes With The Following Benefits


Engage in a collaborative and dynamic work culture
Opportunity to work in a leading digital banking firm


This Role Requires You To Have


Delivering impactful analytics insights
Strategic thinking for converting objectives into analytical plans
Advanced data analysis, statistical modeling, and data visualization
Proficiency in SQL and analytics tools like Looker


You Would Benefit From Having


Experience with dbt, GitHub, and Python
Understanding of product development and agile environments


Lunar values a culture centered around challenge and innovation. They maintain a lively vibe reflecting in their work ethic. Candidates may need to provide or grant access to their criminal record dependent on employment regulations.

Title: Student Assistant for Market Analysis & Forecasting
Place: København SV, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you a curious, analytical thinker with a passion for data-driven insights? If so, we have an exciting opportunity for you!

Join our team at Everllence in Copenhagen, and become directly involved in business development and forecasts in one of the world’s leading engineering companies. As part of our team, you will conduct data analysis, forecasting, and reporting on the global economy, particularly within the international shipping market.

This role offers flexible working hours (8-20 hours per week).

You will play an important role in:


Supporting market research and analysis across the global shipping market and broader macroeconomic developments
Data extraction, transformation, and loading, including data visualization and automation
Developing and maintaining Power BI reports
Maintaining and enhancing SQL databases
Preparing and presenting impactful PowerPoint presentations
Supporting the team and wider organization with ad-hoc analytical tasks and projects


We imagine that you:


Are in the final stage of your Bachelor’s degree or at the beginning of your Master’s within Business, Economics, Data Analytics, Finance, or a related field
Have strong analytical skills and attention to detail
Are proficient in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel
Are familiar with, or have an interest in, SQL (or similar), databases, and data visualization tools such as Power BI
Have strong written and verbal communication skills in English
Work effectively both independently and in a team setting


Welcome to Market Analysis & Forecasting

You will join the Market Analysis & Forecasting team within the Finance function, where our work forms the foundation for key business decisions. We develop forecasts that support budget planning and assess market potential for business case development and the introduction of new products and technologies.

We are responsible for providing the organization with a continuous view of what is shaping the shipping industry - covering everything from fuel prices and freight rates to geopolitical developments, emerging technologies, competitive dynamics, and broader macroeconomic trends.

The team is small and collaborative, working closely together across tasks and topics, while maintaining daily dialogue with other departments on a range of projects. Our team reports directly to the local Chief Financial Officer of Everllence, Denmark.

What can you expect from us:


We offer a competitive salary and pension package
Flexible working hours and the possibility to work from home
An international and collaborative workplace with supportive colleagues and a positive team environment
Opportunities to learn, grow, and gain valuable experience for your future career, with access to relevant courses and training as needed
The opportunity to shape insights that drive real decisions and create tangible business impact, in close collaboration with multiple department across the organization


Employee well-being is an integral part of our culture, with opportunities to take part in a range of social activities - from sports and outdoor events to creative initiatives. We support a healthy work-life balance and mental well-being including health insurance and focus on work atmosphere, offering opportunities for both professional and personal development.

Are you a student ready to apply your theoretical knowledge in practice?

We offer you the opportunity to connect your education with valuable practical experience in a large international company, where diversity and inclusion drive innovation, enabling us to create cutting-edge energy solutions.

At Everllence we have a Student Network created by students for students. Its purpose is to connect students across Denmark, fostering strong academic and social relationships across various departments. This network also offers an opportunity to expand academic connections, potentially paving the way for a future career at Everllence.

In 2027, we will consolidate our Zealand locations into one large site in Trekroner, Roskilde.

Build your future with us

Please apply before Thursday, the 30th of April, 2026. Interviews will be held during the application period. Questions? You are welcome to contact Jeppe Nørregaard Drejer, Market Analyst,

at +45 40503323 or e-mail jeppe.drejer@everllence.com .

We aim for a fair and inclusive recruitment process. Regardless of gender, age, religion, disability, or ethnic background, we encourage you to apply. We embrace a global mindset and intercultural environment to ensure a collaborative and inclusive workplace. If you need support, such as assistive tools for dyslexia, we can assist you.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Hedehusene, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Req Number: 111959

Time Type: Full Time

We’re looking for a Data Analyst who brings structure and clarity to how data is understood, aligned, and connected across an Enterprise Data Platform. You take ownership of how business concepts, source data, and platform models are mapped end-to-end, ensuring consistency, reuse, and clarity across domains and products.

This role is for a hands-on specialist who raises the bar on data analysis quality, documentation, collaboration, and who remains effective in a fast-moving, complex delivery environment where priorities evolve. Working at the core of major digital programs, you ensure analytical work is rigorous, aligned, and fit for reuse – driving value sprint by sprint.

Sounds like you? If so – join us and help set the standard for how data supports real enterprise impact.

About Your Role

Anchored in Global IT, Enterprise Business Development drives some of the most transformative digital initiatives in DSV – connecting data, intelligence, and workflows across divisions, systems, and geographies. In this role, you will work closely with Domain Squad Leads, architects, and Chapter Leads to translate needs into aligned, cross-domain solutions. You ensure capabilities are scalable, interoperable, and built for the long term – while delivering tangible value in every sprint.

Key Responsibilities:



Support squads in complex data analysis and stakeholder situations, helping resolve ambiguities in source data, definitions, and ownership.
Strengthen collaboration and ways of working between business, data, and engineering teams by acting as a bridge between business meaning and technical implementation.
Validate and challenge data requirements and mappings to ensure alignment with the enterprise data platform vision, data mesh principles, and overall company strategy.
Collaborate closely with solution architects, information architects, subject matter experts, business analysts, and squad leads to establish strong feedback loops that ensure end-to-end coherence, data quality, and semantic consistency.
Perform in-depth data profiling and analysis to uncover patterns, anomalies, and inconsistencies in data.
Develop and execute data validation and verification processes to document and improve data integrity and quality.
Create detailed reports and visualizations to communicate data profiling results and insights.



Qualifications

You are a trusted Data Analyst with strong analytical skills and the ability to navigate competing priorities in a complex, global, and matrix organisation. You are skilled at engaging stakeholders across both business and technical communities. You bring experience from large-scale agile delivery environments, you are comfortable supporting multiple squads in parallel, and you excel at translating business needs into clear, actionable requirements that enable scalable solutions.

To thrive and succeed in this role, you have:



2-5 years of experience in roles such as Mapping Analyst, Data Analyst, Data Scientist or similar senior positions.
Proven ability to engage business and data stakeholders to capture business definitions, source data semantics, and transformation logic, and translate them into precise and usable data insights.
Experience working closely with data engineers, solution architects, information architects, and product owners in data platform or analytics environments.
Good knowledge of data platforms, data warehousing or lakehouse concepts, business intelligence, and data transformation patterns. Exposure to logistics, supply chain, or transport domains is beneficial, but not required.
Experience with data profiling tools (e.g. Databricks, or similar).
Familiarity with data quality frameworks and concepts such as data cleansing, data governance, and master data management.
Knowledge of programming languages like Python or R for data analysis (preferred).
Knowledge of cloud systems (e.g. AWS or Azure) and experience in software development is an advantage.



Ready to take the next step?

We accept applications until 30th April 2026, but we screen candidates on an ongoing basis – so we recommend applying as early as possible. No cover letter is needed; just include a brief motivation in your CV.

If you would like to know more about the role, you are welcome to contact Senior Strategy Manager, Catalin Popescu, on constantin.c.popescu@dsv.com

DSV – Global transport and logistics

Working at DSV means playing in a different league.

As a global leader in transport and logistics, we have been on an extraordinary journey of growth. Let’s grow together as we continue to innovate, digitalise and build on our achievements.

With close to 150,000 colleagues in over 90 countries, we work every day to offer solid services and meet our customers’ needs and help them achieve their goals. We know that the best way to achieve this is by bringing in new talent, fresh perspectives and ambitious individuals like you.

At DSV, performance is in our DNA. We don’t just work – we aim to shape the future of logistics. This ambition fuels a dynamic environment built on collaboration with world-class team players, accountability and action. We value inclusivity, embrace different cultures and respect the dignity and rights of every individual. If you want to make an impact, be trusted by customers and grow your career in a forward-thinking company – this is the place to be.

Start here. Go anywhere

Visit dsv.com and follow us on LinkedIn and Facebook.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Place: Billund, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

We are looking for an Analytics Engineer to join our Innovation & Automation group within Analytics & Insights. You'll be building evaluation and governance systems for real products that power commercial decisions – forecasting, pricing, campaign optimisation.

This is a team of four, building from scratch – with strong backing from Analytics & Insights leadership and close collaboration with key partners like the Data Office and Digital Product teams. The standards, the evaluation frameworks, the governance practices, the tooling – all of it is being shaped now, and you'd be part of shaping it.

Analytics & Insights - Innovation & Automation Team sits at the centre of a three-team value chain in M&C Analytics Analytics Engineering builds the data foundation, we build the AI and automation capability engine, Analytics Interface delivers the business products.

Come and join us in Billund at one of the most loved brands in the world where you can have maximum impact!

****Please apply with an English CV****

****No relocation support offered****


Core Responsibilities





Build evaluation frameworks for LLM and agent outputs automated benchmarks, structured regression suites, and human-in-the-loop review where ground-truth datasets don't exist.
Instrument observability into AI systems – accuracy monitoring, drift detection, and clear connection between system behaviour and business outcomes.
Design deployment pipelines for AI products – model serving endpoints, agent workflows, and LLM-based applications on Databricks.
Shape AI governance through production quality gates, lifecycle management, and systematic review that makes reliability an engineering discipline, not a compliance exercise.
Build reusable templates, CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Declarative Automation Bundles), and composable platform components that make the right way to build also the fastest way.
Build and ship AI products from architecture through production monitoring, partnering with Digital Product teams and the Data Office where scope overlaps.
Support testing and onboarding of emerging platform capabilities with the Data Office establishing adoption standards for analytics




Do You Have What It Takes





Strong Python engineering clean, typed, production-ready code as a default.
SQL proficiency writing efficient queries, understanding data models, working with analytical datasets.
Experience working with REST APIs as a consumer.
Experience with model or pipeline lifecycle management – experiment tracking, deployment, monitoring, not just training.
Hands-on experience operating AI/ML or data systems in production – you've kept something running, not just launched it.
Proven data engineering foundations data quality, pipeline reliability, governance of technical standards.
CI/CD and infrastructure-as-code exposure you've built or maintained deployment pipelines, not just used them.




Nice to have





The Databricks stack Unity Catalog, Workflows, MLFlow, Model Serving, Mosaic AI.
PostgreSQL or other relational databases for structured data systems.
LLM-based or agentic systems evaluation of generative outputs, structured tool use, prompt engineering for production.
CI/CD and infrastructure-as-code GitHub Actions, declarative infrastructure management (Terraform, Declarative Automation Bundles, or equivalent).
Experience building internal developer tooling, reusable templates, or platform component consumed by other teams




Join the global LEGO® team

What’s in it for you? Here are some of what to expect





Family Care Leave – We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.
Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.
Wellness – We want you to be your best self, so you’ll have access to the Headspace App and lots of wellbeing initiatives and programmes run by local teams where you are based
Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.
Bonus – We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached, you’ll be rewarded through our global bonus scheme.
Your workplace – Our offices will be your primary workplace with flexibility to work from home during your working week. Your People Leader will discuss the principles of our hybrid work model, role eligibility and frequency with you.

Title: Student Assistant
Place: Kongens Lyngby, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Student Assistant – Portfolio Data Analyst

Are you passionate about data analytics and looking to apply your skills in a real business setting? Do you enjoy working with data and turning it into clear insights? Then you might be our new Student Assistant in Hempel’s Innovation Portfolio Management team!

What can you look forward to in this role?

We work with innovation and new product development, ensuring structure, transparency, and strong decision-making across our portfolio. As a Student Assistant, you will support how we work with data and help transform it into insights used for decision-making.

As part of the team, you will support data management and analysis activities across projects. You will work closely with the team to ensure data quality, build reporting, and contribute to improving how we use data in Portfolio Management.

What will your responsibilities be?


Support Portfolio Management reporting and analysis
Assist with maintaining and improving data quality
Help structure and clean data across different sources
Assist in improving processes and ways of working within Portfolio Management
Ad hoc analysis and support


What do we look for in you?


You are currently studying a relevant degree
Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail
Good skills in Excel (experience with Power BI is a plus)
Interest in working with data and turning it into insights
Structured and able to manage multiple tasks


What do we offer you?

You will join a collaborative and supportive team where you will gain hands-on experience with data analytics. We offer a flexible working environment with an office based in Kgs. Lyngby.

Ready to join us?

Come and be a valued part of a close-knit team based in Kgs. Lyngby, working around 20 hours per week with flexibility to match your academic schedule.

To apply for this position, please submit your application letter with a CV in English through our recruitment system by clicking the "Apply" link at the top. For questions regarding the role, email peham@hempel.com

Please note: we do not accept applications via email due to GDPR. If your email contains personal information, it will be disregarded.

We value diversity and encourage applications from all qualified individuals. Applications will be treated with respect and you will be contacted if we see a potential match.

Application due

2026-04-30

Seniority Level

Internship

Job Functions

Administrative, Project Management

Industry

Chemicals

At Hempel, you’re welcomed to a global community of +7000 colleagues around the world. A workplace built on shared trust, mutual respect and support for each other.

We’re on a mission to double our impact. To succeed, we need bright minds and team players. Which is why, from day one, your input, ideas and initiative are not only welcomed, but expected. In return, you will enjoy great opportunities for development in a growing global company – and be part of the solution by contributing to our global impact.

At Hempel, we are committed to everyone feeling safe, valued and treated equally, in an environment where each employee can bring their authentic selves to work.  We believe that inclusion is key to innovation and by bringing together the most diverse perspectives and talents, we can achieve great things, together.

Title: 12 x Data Portfolio Analysts
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About the job:

Our client is on a journey to strengthen its enterprise data capabilities by driving data value, ensuring data is consumable, secure, private, accurate, available, and usable across the organisation. To accelerate this journey, we are establishing a department with four new domain-specific Data Portfolio Teams, each responsible for executing strategic data initiatives that enable business outcomes within the Market Data, Operations, Counterpart Risk, and Market Risk areas. The data portfolio teams will be supported by a data architect.

The Data Portfolio Analyst is responsible for independently analyzing, identifying, and implementing data quality improvements and controls within a specific pre-defined data portfolio area, in collaboration with data domain owners, data stewards, and key business stakeholders.

Main Responsibilities:


Support domain teams in defining and managing Critical Data Elements (CDEs) and governance requirements within their assigned data portfolio areas.
Assess the current state of data quality for critical data elements (CDEs), with help from data owners, data stewards, and data consumers.
Ensure data quality rules are identified, risks assessed, and appropriate target thresholds defined.
Identify optimal approaches for resolving data quality or consistency issues to achieve targets.
Develop expertise in the Group's Data Quality tool to support rule building in a consistent manner, in your specific domain area of expertise.
Monitor and track ongoing data fidelity (e.g., quality and consistency) levels and metrics that assess adherence to data governance policies.
Ensure data cleansing activities are allocated for the correction of data quality flaws that cannot be addressed fully by automated means.
Monitor delivery health and escalate blockers or issues in your specific domain area of expertise.


Key Requirements:


Strong understanding of how data enables business processes and how data quality impacts operational, regulatory, and customer outcomes.
Good level of understanding with data governance and data quality risk management best practices, ideally experience within financial services or market data.
Solid data analysis skills in SQL and Python; experience with Databricks is beneficial.
Ability to navigate complex data models and understand cross-domain dependencies.
Strong coordination and communication skills, with experience structuring and driving data quality initiatives at different management levels.
Skilled in facilitation and conflict resolution, especially when balancing competing requirements across functions.
Ability to drive behavioral change and support adoption of data governance practices and data-driven ways of working.


Nice to Have:


Experience working in a collaborative team environment.
Familiarity with data visualization tools.
Certification in data governance or data management.


Start: 1st of May (requirement)

Contract duration: 3 secured months, with the potential (probable) extension to 6 months total.

Workload: Fulltime

Location: Consultant shall be able to work onsite from Greater Copenhagen min. 4 days per week and 1 day remote.

Title: Data Annotator
Place: Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Innodata (NASDAQ: INOD) is a leading data engineering company. With more than 2,000 customers and operations in 13 cities around the world, we are an AI technology solutions provider-of-choice for 4 out of 5 of the world’s biggest technology companies, as well as leading companies across financial services, insurance, technology, law, and medicine.

By combining advanced machine learning and artificial intelligence (ML/AI) technologies, a global workforce of subject matter experts, and a high-security infrastructure, we’re helping usher in the promise of AI. Innodata offers a powerful combination of both digital data solutions and easy-to-use, high-quality platforms.




Our global workforce includes over 5,000 employees in the United States, Canada, United Kingdom, the Philippines, India, Sri Lanka, Israel and Germany.




About the Role




Job Title: Data Annotator

Employment Type: Freelance / Project-Based

Location: Remote (Work From Home)

Work Hours: Flexible; minimum commitment of 4–5 hours per day

Eligibility: Open to candidates with a Bachelor’s degree







We are seeking Data Annotator to support a data annotation and linguistic quality project aimed at training Large Language Models (LLMs). This is a freelance, remote opportunity that offers flexible working hours.

The role involves understanding cultural and linguistic nuances, reviewing and annotating data, and following detailed project-specific guidelines to enhance AI performance and accuracy.




Key Responsibilities




Perform data annotation, and text evaluation in accordance with project-specific guidelines.
Ensure high-quality, accurate, and contextually relevant annotations to support Large Language Model (LLM) training.
Review, validate, and refine AI-generated content for grammatical, semantic, and contextual accuracy.
Maintain consistency and quality across all deliverables while adhering to established project standards and timelines.
Collaborate effectively with the project team to provide feedback and contribute to continuous improvement.
Maintain strict confidentiality and comply with all project deadlines and quality assurance protocols.




Required Skills & Qualifications




Bachelor’s degree in mandatory.
Good proficiency in the English language
Prior experience in data annotation, linguistic evaluation, or AI training projects.




What Makes This Role Exciting




.Fully remote opportunity, allowing you to work comfortably from home.
Flexible working hours (4–5 hours per day) to suit your schedule.
The chance to contribute to cutting-edge AI and Large Language Model (LLM) training initiatives, shaping the future of language technology.




Note:

For this role, you are required to complete the English assessment within two (2) days from the date it is assigned to you.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About Us

At IQVIA, our Nordic public health team collaborates closely with clients to co-develop data-driven solutions that enhance patient outcomes and deliver tangible value to the healthcare sector. We possess in-depth knowledge of health data and technology as well as a thorough understanding of our clients’ needs and business objectives. Our approach is grounded in dialogue, co-creation, and sustained partnership.

We are looking for candidates to reinforce the team. In the role you will partner directly with clients to transform complex healthcare data into actionable insights and architect robust platforms that underpin our solutions.

Why Join Us?


Engage in purpose-driven work shaping the future of public healthcare collaboratively with our clients.
Thrive in a supportive, inclusive environment emphasizing ongoing client engagement and partnership.
Drive innovation by advancing AI and analytics within healthcare, involving clients as co-developers.
Utilize modern tools (Power BI, SAP, Hyperion, Azure, .NET, cloud-native technologies) with a focus on your professional growth and work-life balance.
Become part of a Nordic community in a global organization with a strong healthcare professional environment.
Joining happy and dedicated colleagues, a great lunch arrangement, as well as the possibility for flexibility and working from home to achieve a good work-life balance.


Your Role

You will translate client business requirements into insights dashboards, and reports supporting their strategic and operational goals. Close collaboration with clients across all project phases ensures solutions deliver measurable value.

As a Senior Consultant, responsibilities further include designing scalable analytics and AI solutions with an emphasis on quality, compliance, and maintainability.

What You’ll Do


The Business Analyst will, together with our clinical roles, analyze healthcare data needs, map processes, and ensure business requirements are accurately translated into data‑driven solutions.
Develop and deliver BI dashboards and reports (Power BI, Excel) that distill complex datasets into executive-level insights.
Design data models, ETL/ELT pipelines, and semantic layers (e.g., SQL, SSIS/ADF, tabular models) for high-quality analytics.
Construct AI-enabled forecasting, risk/quality monitoring, and NLP summarization workflows; operationalize models using MLOps.
Implement data quality controls (rules, alerts, lineage, audits) and performance monitoring dashboards.


Qualifications


Bachelor’s/Master’s degree in Finance, Mathematics, Data/Computer Science, or related field.
Experience in analytics, data science, data warehousing, AI / Machine Learning or BI, preferably in healthcare or regulated environments.
Skilled in e.g. Power BI, Python, SAS, Excel and the use of SQL.
Demonstrated stakeholder engagement, mentorship, and delivery expertise in regulated data environments.
Domain expertise and experience from healthcare will be an advantage


What We Offer


A supportive, innovative work environment with international exposure.
Training and mentorship to foster professional development.
Competitive compensation and benefits package.
A mission-driven workplace leading AI-enabled analytics and platform engineering.
Opportunities for professional certification and career progression.
Flexible working arrangements and respect for work-life balance.


IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com

IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.

HTML:
About Us

At IQVIA, our Nordic public health team collaborates closely with clients to co-develop data-driven solutions that enhance patient outcomes and deliver tangible value to the healthcare sector. We possess in-depth knowledge of health data and technology as well as a thorough understanding of our clients’ needs and business objectives. Our approach is grounded in dialogue, co-creation, and sustained partnership.

We are looking for candidates to reinforce the team. In the role you will partner directly with clients to transform complex healthcare data into actionable insights and architect robust platforms that underpin our solutions.

Why Join Us?

  • Engage in purpose-driven work shaping the future of public healthcare collaboratively with our clients.
  • Thrive in a supportive, inclusive environment emphasizing ongoing client engagement and partnership.
  • Drive innovation by advancing AI and analytics within healthcare, involving clients as co-developers.
  • Utilize modern tools (Power BI, SAP, Hyperion, Azure, .NET, cloud-native technologies) with a focus on your professional growth and work-life balance.
  • Become part of a Nordic community in a global organization with a strong healthcare professional environment.
  • Joining happy and dedicated colleagues, a great lunch arrangement, as well as the possibility for flexibility and working from home to achieve a good work-life balance.

Your Role

You will translate client business requirements into insights dashboards, and reports supporting their strategic and operational goals. Close collaboration with clients across all project phases ensures solutions deliver measurable value.

As a Senior Consultant, responsibilities further include designing scalable analytics and AI solutions with an emphasis on quality, compliance, and maintainability.

What You’ll Do

  • The Business Analyst will, together with our clinical roles, analyze healthcare data needs, map processes, and ensure business requirements are accurately translated into data‑driven solutions.
  • Develop and deliver BI dashboards and reports (Power BI, Excel) that distill complex datasets into executive-level insights.
  • Design data models, ETL/ELT pipelines, and semantic layers (e.g., SQL, SSIS/ADF, tabular models) for high-quality analytics.
  • Construct AI-enabled forecasting, risk/quality monitoring, and NLP summarization workflows; operationalize models using MLOps.
  • Implement data quality controls (rules, alerts, lineage, audits) and performance monitoring dashboards.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s/Master’s degree in Finance, Mathematics, Data/Computer Science, or related field.
  • Experience in analytics, data science, data warehousing, AI / Machine Learning or BI, preferably in healthcare or regulated environments.
  • Skilled in e.g. Power BI, Python, SAS, Excel and the use of SQL.
  • Demonstrated stakeholder engagement, mentorship, and delivery expertise in regulated data environments.
  • Domain expertise and experience from healthcare will be an advantage

What We Offer

  • A supportive, innovative work environment with international exposure.
  • Training and mentorship to foster professional development.
  • Competitive compensation and benefits package.
  • A mission-driven workplace leading AI-enabled analytics and platform engineering.
  • Opportunities for professional certification and career progression.
  • Flexible working arrangements and respect for work-life balance.

IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com

IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen Metropolitan Area
Description:
Data Engineer indenfor Lakehouse-miljø tilbydes moderne platform.




Krüger Search søger en teknisk stærk ”hands-on” Dataingeniør, som kan få data til at virke i praksis hos Brdr. A&O Johansen A/S i Albertslund.




Du vil være med til at bygge og drifte en moderne dataplatform baseret på streaming og lakehouse-teknologi.
Du kommer til at arbejde med moderne datastak og eksisterende on-premises løsninger.
Du vil have fokus på implementering, drift og stabilitet, mindre på arkitekturdesign og dokumentation.
Dit fokus vil være på det eksekverende og driftsnære.
Vi søger en praktisk & detaljeorienteret Dataingeniør, som får stor teknisk indflydelse.
Du får stor handlefrihed, en varieret hverdag og spændende opgaver.
Du får en stærk udviklingsplatform både personligt og fagligt.
Du får et godt, uformelt og åbent arbejdsklima med god teamånd.
Du får en velrenommeret arbejdsplads med gode & ordnede forhold.
Du får en grundig introduktion til stillingen & virksomheden.
Du får en meget kompetent og samarbejdsorienteret chef.







Brdr. A & O Johansen A/S:

Brdr. A&O Johansen A/S er en af Danmarks største håndværkergrossister. Se venligst www.ao.dk.
Der bliver løbende investeret i digitalisering og effektivisering af forretningsprocesserne i virksomheden, hvor IT prioriteres meget højt.
Virksomheden omsætter for 6 mia.kr, og har over 1100 medarbejdere, heraf 35 på Århus kontoret.
Meget åbent og uformelt arbejdsklima, stor vidensdeling, god korpsånd og engageret stemning.
3-dobbelt vinder af Bedste B2B-virksomhed samt
3-dobbelt vinder af Bedste Omnichannel virksomhed ved uddelingen af E-Handelsprisen.




Du tilbydes:

En attraktiv lønpakke efter kvalifikationer.
God pension.
Fuldtids stilling.
Gode muligheder for hjemmearbejde efter nærmere aftale.
Støtte til hjemmearbejdsplads.
Mobil, Laptop.
God kantine.
Meget gode muligheder for videreuddannelse efter nærmere aftale.
Meget gode faglige og personlige udviklingsmuligheder.
Gode sociale aktiviteter.







Dine opgaver er:

Deltage i at bygge & drifte dataplatform.
Dataplatform baseret på streaming & lakehouse-teknologi.
Ansvar for udvikling og drift af Kafka-baserede data pipelines.
Sikre data stilles til rådighed som self-service, i praksis.
Arbejde med Apache Iceberg i en lakehouse-arkitektur.
Datatransformation, validering og schema-håndtering.
Performanceoptimering og fejlsøgning i produktionsmiljøer.
Eksponering af data til BI og applikationer (SQL / streams).
Vedligeholdelse og videreudvikling af on-premises dataintegrationer.
Håndtering af komplekse dataflows og fejlsøgning.
Erfaring med & interesse for både ny teknologi og legacy-miljøer.
Løbende dialog med interessenter.
Løbende vidensdeling med team & afdeling.







Din nærmeste chef:

Giver stor frihed under ansvar, faglig dygtig & erfaren.
Venlig, åben, dialogorienteret og kompetent.







IT afdelingen:

Veldrevet & Professionel IT Afdeling.
IT afdelingen består af 55 medarbejdere.
Meget uformelt, dialogorienteret, åbent og dynamisk miljø.
Stor rummelighed.
Stor vidensdeling og engagement i udviklingsteamet og resten af IT afdelingen.
Meget korte kommandoveje.
IT afdelingen tilpasses løbende, med gode muligheder for spændende og udfordrende opgaver til følge.
Tæt samarbejde med hele Brdr. A&O Johansen A/S koncernen.







Dine personlige kvalifikationer:

Teknisk & praktisk tænkende, realistisk, teamplayer, positiv, flink & venlig, ansvarsbevidst, nysgerrig, løsnings- & dialogorienteret.







Dine erhvervskvalifikationer:

Erfaring som Data Engineer.
Erfaring med praktiske ”hands-on” opgaver som Data Engineer.
Erfaring med Data pipelines i produktion – ikke kun proof of concepts.
Erfaring med Apache Kafka i produktion (ikke kun teoretisk kendskab).
Erfaring med Apache Iceberg eller tilsvarende lakehouse-teknologi.
Gerne erfaring med Apache Flink eller anden stream processing.
Erfaring med MS SQL.
Erfaring med drift, stabilitet og ansvar for løsninger i produktion.
Erfaring med SSIS på MS SQL Server (on-premises)
Erfaring med data warehouse.
Erfaring med ETL.
Erfaring med hybridarkitekturer (on-premises + moderne dataplatform).
Erfaring med håndtering af komplekse dataflows og fejlsøgning.
Erfaring med legacy-miljøer og løbende følger teknologiudviklingen.
Erfaring med hands-on løsning af praktiske/tekniske udfordringer.
Vant til at have stor teknisk indflydelse.
God forretningsforståelse.
Taler & forstår dansk på professionelt niveau.







Uddannelseskvalifikationer:

Datalogi, Cand. IT, Ingeniør, Civilingeniør eller lignende med relevant erfaring.
Anden uddannelsesbaggrund med relevant erfaring.
Autodidakt med relevant erfaring.







Geografisk placering:

Albertslund, tættere på store indfaldsveje samt tog- og busforbindelse.







Ansøgningsfrist:

Snarest muligt.
Vi behandler ansøgninger løbende.







Tiltrædelse:

Snarest eller efter nærmere aftale.







Interesseret i stillingen:

Alle kvalificerede kandidater, uanset køn, kønsidentitet, seksuel orientering, etnicitet, religion, handicap eller alder er velkommen til at søge.
Send os venligst dit cv mrk. GDI via ANSØGNING
Har du spørgsmål, er du meget velkommen til at skrive til: krugersearch@kruger-search.com

Title: Product Analyst (all genders)
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
The Role (What’s In The Box)

It’s a typical day in our Islands Brygge office. Data is being pulled, patterns are analyzed, and decisions are made on which recipes make it into the meal kits we send to thousands of customers every week. As a Product Analyst (all genders) in our Menu Strategy & Planning team, you use those insights to inform the development of our recipe database, a key component in how our physical product comes together.

Along The Way, a Simple Question Comes Up

What actually makes a recipe one that customers come back for?

It’s exactly this kind of question you will help answer. Your mission is to use your analytical expertise to facilitate the development of a broad pool of diverse, well-liked, and affordable recipes to excite and delight our customers, while supporting the planning needs of our menu.

What You’ll Do (The Recipe)


Analyze customer and product data to generate insights and provide strategic direction for the development of our culinary product, ensuring a strong balance across cost, utilization, variety, retention, and performance
Translate product and menu composition strategy into clear and aligned recipe development plans
Manage the launch of product improvements (e.g. recipe concept launches) to our customers
Continuously improve and optimize recipe planning processes, ensuring strong coordination across teams (menu planning, editorial, recipe developers, and product marketing)
Contribute to high customer engagement by optimizing core menu signaletics and communication (e.g. tags, labels, categories, CRM)
Build and maintain a network of international counterparts to stay up to date with product planning best practices and collaborate across markets


What You’ll Bring (Your Ingredients)


Have ~2 years of hands-on data analysis experience, with strong SQL skills that enable you to uncover insights
Have strong problem-solving skills that help you find the right approach to tackle any analytical challenge
Easily find your way around spreadsheets and have experience working with Excel or Gsheet to efficiently merge, transform, visualize, and analyze data
Enjoy collaborating with peers and stakeholders, leveraging your analytics skills to support non-technical colleagues in their creative work
Are self-driven and can work independently to drive progress in your area always keeping an eye on quality and timeliness
Have strong analytical skills enabling you to identify patterns in large data sets, organize and make sense of multiple data sources
Have strong business acumen allowing you to identify and prioritize high-impact business opportunities within recipe planning


Above all, we are looking for people who will make HelloFresh better. There are many different ways of developing skills, and we love diverse experiences! So even if you don’t “tick all the boxes” but think you’d thrive in this role, we would really like to learn more about you.

What We Offer (The Toppings)


HelloFresh Discounts – Enjoy 65% off your HelloFresh Meal Box and 40% off Factor_ subscriptions.
Learning and Development – Make the most of a dedicated Learning & Development budget and the HelloMint mentorship program.
Receive 25 days of paid vacation per year, + 5 days of feriefridage. Paid leave also provided for your child’s first sick day.
Parent Benefits – Benefit from a Parental Leave policy supporting your family needs.
Compensation and Perks – Includes a 5% company-paid pension and insurance package.
Health – Access to Spill online therapy, available within 24 hours of request.
Daily Comforts – Office perks include drinks and snacks, subsidized catered lunches, and breakfast every Friday.
Fitness and Well-being – Discounts at SATS gym, plus access to sports and exercise clubs.


Are you the missing ingredient?

If this sounds like a tasty opportunity, we’d be excited to hear from you. We aim to review your profile and respond within 5 business days.

Title: Freelance Data Science Engineer (Python & SQL)
Place: Denmark
Description:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:


Design original computational data science problems that simulate real-world analytical workflows across industries (telecom, finance, government, e-commerce, healthcare)
Create problems requiring Python programming to solve (using Pandas, Numpy, Scipy, Sklearn, Statsmodels, Matplotlib, Seaborn)
Ensure problems are computationally intensive and cannot be solved manually within reasonable timeframes (days/weeks)
Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains in data processing, statistical analysis, feature engineering, predictive modeling, and insight extraction
Create deterministic problems with reproducible answers: avoid stochastic elements or require fixed random seeds for exact reproducibility
Base problems on real business challenges: customer analytics, risk assessment, fraud detection, forecasting, optimization, and operational efficiency
Design end-to-end problems spanning the complete data science pipeline (data ingestion → cleaning → EDA → modeling → validation → deployment considerations)
Incorporate big data processing scenarios requiring scalable computational approaches
Verify solutions using Python with standard data science libraries and statistical methods
Document problem statements clearly with realistic business contexts and provide verified correct answers


What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for Data Science specialists with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:


5+ years of hands-on data science experience with proven business impact
Portfolio of completed projects and publications showcasing real-world problem-solving
Expert Python programming for data science (pandas, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, statsmodels)
Expert statistical analysis and machine learning - deep understanding of algorithms, methods, and their practical applications
Expert with SQL and database operations for data manipulation and analysis
Experience with GenAI technologies (LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, vector databases)
Understanding of MLOps practices and model deployment workflows
Knowledge of modern frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain)
Strong written English (C1+)


How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $58 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Business Insights Analyst
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Business Insights Analyst, North & Central Europe

Do you enjoy turning complex data into clear business value? You could be our next Business Insights Analyst, helping shape commercial decisions across North & Central Europe through strong analytics and CRM expertise.

Your new role

As Business Insights Analyst, you will combine business analytics with regional CRM system ownership to ensure high-quality data, stable system operations and actionable insights. Reporting to the Regional Business Insights Lead, you will play a key role in supporting Commercial and Medical teams across the region.

You will own and optimize CRM and related systems, contribute to a new CRM implementation in close collaboration with global functions, and translate data into insights that drive decision-making. The role offers a strong mix of system ownership, stakeholder engagement and analytical work, giving you broad exposure across both affiliate and global levels.

Your future team

Business Insights, North & Central Europe is a collaborative and stakeholder-focused function supporting regional performance and decision-making. You will work closely with colleagues across Commercial, Medical, affiliates and global teams, as well as external consultants.

The team values curiosity, strong collaboration and a practical approach to problem-solving. You will engage regularly with global CRM communities and regional stakeholders, contributing to shared solutions and continuous improvement.

The position is based in Valby, Denmark. This role is primarily office-based, with the option of working from home up to 2 days per week.

What You Bring To The Team

You bring solid experience within business analytics and systems, combined with a structured and proactive mindset. You are comfortable working across multiple stakeholders and enjoy translating data into meaningful insights.

You likely bring:


A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, Statistics or a related field
5–8 years of experience in Business Analysis, Business Intelligence, Sales Operations or Commercial IT (pharma experience is an advantage)
Strong CRM experience (Veeva preferred) and experience with BI tools such as Power BI, Qlik or Tableau
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills



As a person, you are self-driven and take initiative, with strong project management and problem-solving skills. You communicate clearly, collaborate effectively across functions, and enjoy engaging with stakeholders at both regional and global levels.

Our promise to you

Lundbeck offers an inspiring workplace and innovative culture, where our curiosity, accountability and adaptability enable us to transform lives. We want to go faster and further on addressing the big unmet needs of people living with brain disorders. We offer rewarding careers with a mix of exciting tasks and development opportunities that are balanced with initiatives focused on your well-being.

We need every brain in the game, and at Lundbeck, we are committed to building a workforce that is as diverse as the people we serve. Read more about our commitment at www.lundbeck.com/global/about-us/our-commitment/diversity-and-inclusion

Apply now

Upload your CV and include a few lines about your motivation for applying. We ask you not to include a photo or personal information that does not relate to your professional experience.

Learn more about us at www.lundbeck.com, LinkedIn or Instagram (@h_lundbeck).

#EveryBrainInTheGame

This job ad is intended for individuals seeking a career opportunity with Lundbeck. We engage with recruitment and search firms where needed on the basis of a written agreement, and we do not accept unsolicited requests of any kind. If you work as a recruitment consultant, you are kindly instructed to refrain from contacting Lundbeck or the hiring manager directly with suggested candidates. If you violate this policy, you do so at your own risk and for your own account, and Lundbeck will not assume any liability nor pay any associated fees resulting from such violation.

Title: Profil til data management og ledelsesstøtte i PET's sektion for personundersøgelser
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Et helt centralt element i at opretholde et højt sikkerhedsniveau i Danmark er, at det kræver en sikkerhedsgodkendelse for at få indsigt i klassificeret information. Personundersøgelsessektionen i PETs overordnede opgave er at sikkerhedsundersøge personer ansat i eller arbejdende for offentlige myndigheder inden godkendelsen.

Behovet for sikkerhedsundersøgelser er stigende. Personundersøgelsessektionen i PET har behov for flere gode kræfter, der vil løfte opgaverne sammen med os.

For at understøtte den stigende efterspørgsel har vi oprettet en stilling som fuldmægtig. Dine primære opgaver forestiller vi os vil være fordelt på henholdsvis data management og den resterende del vil være ledelsesunderstøttende opgaver. Opgaver i forhold til data management vil blandt andet være vedligehold og udvikling af den nuværende ledelsesinformation samt at være nøgleperson i forhold til udvikling af dataanvendelsen i vores digitale løsninger. Opgaverne i forhold til ledelsesunderstøttelse vil eksempelvis være udarbejdelse af præsentationer samt større koordineringsopgaver.

Da stillingen er nyoprettet vil der i høj grad være mulighed for at præge indholdet i stillingen og de arbejdsmetoder, der anvendes. Du vil i det daglige referere til sektionslederne for Personundersøgelser.

Sektionen for Personundersøgelsers overordnede mål er at sikre, at personer ansat i eller arbejdende for offentlige myndigheder bliver grundigt undersøgt inden sikkerhedsgodkendelse. Du kan få mere at vide om sikkerhedsundersøgelser her: www.pet.dk/raadgivning-om-sikkerhed/saadan-bliver-du-sikkerhedsgodkendt.

Få en hverdag, der aldrig bliver hverdag

Som ansat i PET bliver du en del af en hverdag, der er præget af opgaver, som ikke findes andre steder, og hver dag byder på nye udfordringer, som ikke fandtes i går. Vi er vidt forskellige, og derfor kan vi gøre en forskel. Det handler om den, du er, og det, du bidrager med.

Selvom vores arbejde er præget af alvoren i det højere formål, er tonen uformel, og du vil opleve et unikt sammenhold på tværs af afdelinger og fagområder. Vi har en hverdag, der aldrig bliver hverdag, og det kan du også få.

Om dig

Vi søger en dygtig og engageret profil, der har interesse for konkret sagsbehandling af personoplysninger og formidling af data på dette. Du kan bruge din gode dømmekraft til at træffe de rette valg og trives med, når vilkårene omkring dig ændres. Du har forståelse for, at PET er en særlig arbejdsplads, og at det kræver noget ekstra at arbejde her. PET er en aktiv del af samfundet, hvorfor din nysgerrighed på omverdenen og nye opgaver er gode forudsætninger for at lykkes.

Fagligt vil vi lægge vægt på, at du har konkret erfaring med lignende opgaver fra tidligere stillinger.

Vi Forestiller Os, At Du Har


Har solide kvantitative analytiske evner
Blik for detaljer og for at data stemmer og er korrekte
Lyst til at arbejde intensivt med Power Bi og Excel
Forståelse for formidling af data - både mundtligt og skriftligt
Evnen til at udfordre og udvikle vores dataanvendelse men at du også ved, hvornår noget er godt nok og dækkende
Stærke formuleringsevner både på skrift og i tale
Har naturligt flair for at strukturere opgaver og holde både overblikket og deadlines
Evnen at udfordre vanetænkning og bidrage med nye perspektiver på en problemstilling
En relevant akademisk baggrund. Måske har du en baggrund inden for data science, statistik, økonomi eller tilsvarende. Har du opnået dine kompetencer ad anden vej, hører vi også gerne fra dig.


I PET vægter vi desuden visse personlige kompetencer højt. Det er kompetencer, som vi mener, er essentielle for alle PET-ansatte uanset faglig baggrund. Det gør vi, fordi det er vigtigt for os, at vi finder det rette match. Du kan læse om kompetencerne her www.pet.dk/job.

Ansættelsesforhold

Stillingen ønskes besat hurtigst muligt.

Du kan læse mere om hele PET’s ansættelsesproces på www.pet.dk/job.

Et ansættelsesforhold i PET skal behandles med diskretion, hvilket du som ansøger skal være opmærksom på. Det betyder, at du som udgangspunkt godt kan fortælle tætte og fortrolige relationer, som du har tillid til, at du har søgt en stilling i PET, men ikke hvad jobbet indebærer.

Det er en forudsætning for ansættelse i PET, at du kan sikkerhedsgodkendes til yderst hemmelig, ligesom du skal kunne opretholde sikkerhedsgodkendelsen gennem hele din ansættelse. Som sikkerhedsgodkendt forventes det, at du er særlig opmærksom på din tavshedspligt i forhold til klassificeret information.

Du bliver ansat på overenskomstvilkår i henhold til de til enhver tid gældende aftaler mellem Finansministeriet og Akademikernes Centralorganisation.

Dit arbejdssted bliver på Klausdalsbrovej 1 i Søborg, lige ved siden af Buddinge station, hvor både S-tog og bus kører lige til døren. I PET arbejder vi på interne systemer og med fortrolige opgaver, og derfor møder vi også på arbejde hver dag for at løse vores opgaver.

Yderligere information

Du er altid meget velkommen til at kontakte os på hverdage på mail hr-rekruttering@politi.dk eller på telefon +45 24 62 18 45, hvis du har spørgsmål til stillingen, PET som arbejdsplads eller lignende.

Når du søger en stilling hos PET, giver du lov til, at vi må indhente dit CPR-nummer via CPR-registeret med henblik på identifikation af dig i forbindelse med rekrutteringsprocessen. Vi indhenter dit CPR-nummer, så vi sikkert kan fremsende relevante og nødvendige dokumenter til dig via Digital Post.

Det er vigtigt for os, at PET afspejler den mangfoldighed, som vi arbejder for. Derfor opfordrer vi alle interesserede, uanset køn, alder, religion, seksualitet og etniske tilhørsforhold til at søge stillingen.

Træd ind i en lukket verden, der åbner nye døre, hvor vi udvikler os, træner og bygger videre på det, vi kan. Om vi udvikler nye systemer, analyserer data eller finjusterer fodstillingen til skydetræning, så er vi altid på tæerne. Det skaber gode muligheder for, at du kan rykke dig med os, vokse med opgaverne og åbne nye døre i dit arbejdsliv.

Title: Student Assistants for Data Analysis and Risk Management
Place: Kongens Lyngby, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you passionate about sustainability and risk management? And do you have a good understanding of how to take data-driven decisions? Then, apply now as a Student Assistant.




You will join the Sustainable Procurement, Real Estate and Third-party Risk team, a collaborative and diverse group of 15 colleagues. Together, we work on developing tools and processes that improve sustainability performance, ensure high-quality environmental data, and drive cross-organisational initiatives related to procurement, office leases, and workplace services.




We are looking for someone who enjoys working with data, is curious about systems and processes, and thrives in a team-oriented environment. You will report to the Head of Sustainable Procurement, Real Estate and Third-party Risk and work from our office in Lyngby, Denmark.




Your main tasks will include: 



Supporting onboarding, follow-up, and analysis of suppliers in relation to risk and ESG assessments using data and business intelligence tools
Developing reports and insights to communicate progress, key findings, and improvement areas to stakeholders
Collaborating with suppliers and internal stakeholders to support corrective action plans
Contributing to the integration of ESG considerations into procurement processes
Assisting with ad hoc projects across teams, providing you with broad, hands-on experience



Your profile




Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree, with a minimum of 1.5 years remaining before final graduation. Your prior exposure to similar tasks through relevant work experience or academic coursework will be beneficial, especially within supply chain and risk management.




As a person, we believe you have an open and curious nature and easily build strong professional relationships. Moreover, you thrive in collaborative environments, while being able to independently take ownership of assignments. Also, you leverage your analytical skills to tackle challenges and maintain a structured and methodical approach to work.




On top of that, you will



Demonstrate solid process and system understanding including the use of MS office systems like Excel and Power Point
Have an interest in data analysis and can work with structured and unstructured data
Have a structured and analytical mindset
Communicate clearly in English (Nordic languages are a plus, but not required)



What we offer



A relevant student job with real responsibility and learning opportunities
Hands-on experience in sustainability, procurement, and risk management
Insight into working with data and business intelligence tools in a professional setting
A supportive and inclusive team environment
A community that offers leisure activities and social events
Flexible working hours (contracted 15 hours per week), with understanding for exam periods. Opportunity to work from home.




 If you are keen to make an impact in our supply chain and grow your professional skills, apply now to join us on this journey!




Please submit your application as soon as possible and no later than 5 May 2026, as we review applications and invite candidates for interviews on an ongoing basis.




A place to work and so much more




At COWI, we work together with our customers to shape a sustainable and liveable world. We do it by applying our knowledge and curiosity – and sometimes even our courage – to create the solutions the world needs today to enable a better tomorrow. That is why we say no to fossil-based projects and aspire to have 100 per cent of our revenue come from activities that move our customers towards sustainability.




We value differences and development and cultivate an environment of belonging and having fun. Because that is what brings out the best in you, at work and at home.




With offices primarily located in Scandinavia, the UK, North America, Poland and India, we are currently 8,000 people who bring their expertise in engineering, architecture, energy and the environment into play.




Got more questions
If you have any questions about the role, feel free to contact Nanna Rosenvinge Vibede at nrvi@cowi.com.




Get to know us even better at our website, www.cowi.com, where you can learn more about our projects, our strategy, what we want to achieve and what life is like at COWI.




We do our utmost to ensure that our recruitment process is as fair and unbiased as possible. You can help us by not including a photo in your CV when you apply.




Diversity is fundamental to COWI, inclusion is key.




COWI provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability or genetics. In addition to federal law requirements, COWI complies with applicable state and local laws governing non-discrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation and training.

We do our utmost to ensure that our recruitment process is as fair and unbiased as possible. You can help us by not including a photo in your CV when you apply.


Diversity is fundamental to COWI, inclusion is key.


COWI provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability or genetics. In addition to federal law requirements, COWI complies with applicable state and local laws governing non-discrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation and training.

Title: Business Analyst - Data & Back-Office Systems - based in Luxembourg
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence. The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi-cultural environment. We also offer relocation support.

The EIB, the European Union's bank, is seeking to recruit for its Finance Directorate (FI), Planning and Settlement of Operations Department (PRO), Contracts and Business Solutions Division (CBS), Business Digitalisation and Support Unit (BuDiS) at its headquarters in Luxembourg, a Business Analyst – Data & Back-Office Systems*.

This is a full-time position at grade 5 for which the EIB offers a permanent contract.


Internal benchmark Officer Business Support for IT Applications


Panel interviews are anticipated for June 2026.

Purpose

You will lead and structure complex business analysis activities to implement and maintain critical software solutions. This role involves providing expertise in business requirements analysis, including data-driven insights, process analysis, solution design and software configuration to meet the organization's digitalization, technology security, and regulatory needs.

Operating Network

You will report to the Head of Unit (HoU). You will be in contact with other colleagues in FI (Finance Directorate) and other Directorates for business aspects, while you will co-operate closely with the other units of the FI-PRO Back-Office Divisions and with GIS on technical aspects.

Regular telework is allowed if the Bank’s telework policy and the Minimum Presence Required rules applicable for FI PRO teams are respected.

Accountabilities

BUSINESS PROJECTS

Provide business support to projects with specific scope and timing to ensure their realization within the set scope, time, and budget.


Act as a Business Analyst on complex business or IT projects in the Lending, Treasury, or Borrowings area (e.g. new product implementation, new functionality introduction, new application, Package upgrades, etc.).
Collaborate in workshops/discussions to understand – within assigned business area – project goals, workflows, complex business needs, gaps, and opportunities.
Review the technical and transactional documentation related to the approved solution before implementation.
Advise the project stakeholders on solution feasibility, risks, and implementation trade-offs.
Report on a regular basis the status of the FI-PRO applications’ developments to the HoU.


PROVIDE SUPPORT AND ENSURE MAINTENANCE AND DEVELOPMENT OF SYSTEMS

Ensure efficient deployment and operation of FI-PRO’s applications within the unit, maintaining reliable financial data and high user satisfaction.


Provide 1st level user support: analyse requests, identify workarounds, provide specific advice and follow-up on resolutions.
Identify recurring issues and propose structural improvements to processes, data flows and reporting.
Gather user requirements for maintenance and developments, follow-up on implementations.
Execute User Acceptance Tests (UAT) test plans to ensure that business requirements and functional specifications are implemented properly and meet objectives, (e.g. review and definition of rules, reports and activities).
Provide status updates to the senior management on the development.
Collaborate to the training sessions organization when required.


CONTACT POINT

Manage relationships and collaborate with counterparts from others EIB Group’s Directorates.


Act as the contact point and represent FI-PRO in working groups and transversal initiatives, when related to the applications under unit’s remit.
Participate and collaborate to NPCs and working groups that impact FI-PRO on new products or bank-wide initiatives. Define solutions that will meet the requirements of the services and cover the business needs.
Streamline the communications with IT and the software vendor for problem resolution.


Qualifications


University degree (minimum an equivalent to a Bachelor) preferably in the fields of information technology, mathematics or finance.
Minimum 5 years of relevant professional experience, ideally in the financial sector, or business consultant companies. Solid proven experience as Business analyst.
Proven experience in data visualization tools (ideally 2 years or more), particularly Power BI, including designing business-facing dashboards, defining semantic data models, and translating business requirements into visual insights.
Good knowledge of financial instruments, systems and processes related to Back-Offices (Borrowings and/or Treasury and/or Lending).
Good understanding of payment market standards including the latest ISO 20022 standards.
Experience with payment and reconciliation software such as ION Wallstreet D&C, SWIFT Alliance Access, Accurate NXG, or similar platforms.
Good presentation and communication skills.
Good coordination skills.
Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office 365.
Experience in IT projects involving business rules automation and IT algorithm implementation, including understanding conditions, loops, and parsing, would be an advantage.
Experience in project management would be considered an advantage.
Knowledge of a financial back-office software (ION Wallstreet TRM - CLM or similar) would be an advantage.
Proficiency in Microsoft Power Platform would be an advantage.
Excellent knowledge of English and/or French (***), with a good command of the other. Knowledge of other EU languages would be an advantage.


Competencies

Find Out More About EIB Core Behavioural Skills Here

To find out more about our eligibility criteria click here

(***) Unless stated explicitly as a required qualification, a good command of French is not a pre-requisite for hire. As both English and French are however official working languages of the EIB, proficiency in both languages is a pre-requisite for your future career development. Any language clause in your contract must be fulfilled in order for you to be eligible for a promotion (either via the annual appraisal cycle or via an internal selection process). Proficiency is understood to mean the attainment of level 5 of the Inter Institutional language courses, corresponding to B1.2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFRL). The Bank offers appropriate training support.

We hire and value talent with unique characteristics, creating a work environment where they can be themselves. We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity.

If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process due to a disability, neurodivergence, or a chronic health condition, please contact the EIB Recruitment team Jobs@eib.org who will manage your request appropriately.

By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation.

Deadline for applications: 6th May 2026
