Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About the company

We are a fast-growing, VC-backed energy-tech scale-up on a mission to connect electric vehicles directly with power markets and make smart charging completely frictionless for consumers. Our fully automated, data-driven platform forecasts energy needs, tracks electricity prices and grid conditions in real time, and optimises charging to maximise savings and unlock new revenue streams for our customers.

You will join a small, highly technical team working at the intersection of real-time data, energy markets and EV charging.




The role

As a Data Engineer, you will own and evolve the data infrastructure at the core of our optimisation platform. You’ll work closely with software engineers and optimisation specialists to ensure models and products are built on trusted, production-grade data systems.

Key responsibilities:

Design, build and operate production data pipelines and data models for time-series and event-driven workloads
Ingest and process external data feeds (market prices, asset telemetry, grid signals) into our core data platform
Improve latency, reliability and observability across the stack to support fast decision-making in live markets
Develop and maintain backtesting and simulation frameworks for new strategies and product features
Contribute to scaling the platform as the number of connected assets and markets grows




Tech stack (current and planned)

Python as the primary language
Cloud data platform (GCP or similar), containerised services and modern CI/CD
Relational databases (e.g. PostgreSQL) and time-series databases (e.g. TimescaleDB or similar)
Streaming / event-driven infrastructure and real-time data processing (e.g. Kafka, Pub/Sub, Kinesis, or equivalent)

You don’t need experience with our exact tools, but you should be comfortable working in a modern cloud/data environment and picking up new technologies.







About you

We imagine you have:

3–6 years of experience as a Data Engineer or similar, working with production-grade data systems
Strong hands-on skills in Python and SQL
Experience building and operating data pipelines for time-series, streaming or event-driven use cases
Solid understanding of reliability, performance, monitoring and testing for data-intensive systems
Experience from energy, trading, fintech, or other real-time, high-stakes domains is a strong plus
Bonus: familiarity with a systems language (e.g. Rust or C++) or with statistical / ML tooling




You’ll thrive here if you:

Enjoy end-to-end ownership of your systems, from design to production
Are pragmatic, curious, and comfortable in a startup/scale-up environment with high autonomy
Want your work to have a direct impact on decarbonisation and the future of electric mobility




What we offer

Competitive salary (50,000 - 55,000 DKK/month) plus 5–15% performance bonus
High degree of autonomy and ownership over core parts of the data platform
Close collaboration with experienced engineers and domain experts in energy and optimisation
Central Copenhagen office, flexible hybrid setup, and an informal, international team culture

Title: Accelerated Computing Research Intern
Place: Roskilde, Region Zealand, Denmark
Description:
NVIDIA is searching for talented interns at the bachelor, master, or PhD level to join our growing software architecture team. The ideal candidates will be conducting innovative research at the intersection of Networking, Security, and Communications, and working alongside top authorities in these fields. With incredible resources at your disposal, you will be able to impact, contribute and advance these exciting domains. Topics include but are not limited to remote direct memory access, hardware offloading and acceleration, AI for networking and security, storage management, cryptography, and architecture. With its unique open culture, NVIDIA is one of the best industry labs to do Accelerated Computing research.

DPU Data-Processing Units are the smallest form of a data center, and provide the foundation to implement accelerated computing, where both processing and networking experience a performance boost through the utilization of dedicated processors. DPUs started out as the engine for accelerated networking, but now they can run AI inference and training on-board, deep packet inspection and cryptography accelerations among others thanks to onboard GPUs and acceleration engines. Join us to research next-generation accelerated data-center architectures during your internship with NVIDIA.

What You'll Be Doing


Be at the forefront of modern data center research
Partner and collaborate with forward-thinking industry leaders and external researchers
Participate and speak at conferences and events
Publish original research
Work with intelligent networking machines powered by AI systems that can learn, reason and interact with other network components


What We Need To See


Currently pursuing a degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Math, Physics, Statistics, or a related field (bachelor, master, or PhD level)
General computer science background, preferably in algorithm design, system programming, networking, operating system, and computer architecture
Strong programming (C/C++/Python) and software development skills
Track record of technical excellence through coursework, projects, publications, or public repositories
Good communication skills


NVIDIA is widely considered to be one of the technology world's most desirable employers. We have some of the most forward-thinking and hardworking people in the world working for us. Are you creative and autonomous? Do you love the challenge of crafting the highest performance & lowest power silicon possible? If so, we want to hear from you. Come, join our Networking Research Team and help build the real-time, cost-effective computing platform driving our success in this exciting and quickly growing field.

Title: Data Student Assistant
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About us:

Templafy is the leading AI-powered document generation platform for companies with a need to create accurate, compliant, and on-brand documents with maximum efficiency and ease. Accessible directly from applications like Microsoft Office, Google Workspace and Salesforce, Templafy is trusted by more than 800 industry leading enterprises, including KPMG, Adobe, and PWC. Our platform simplifies document workflows and eliminates the complexity and risk associated with the content creation process.




Founded in Copenhagen, Denmark, in 2014, Templafy’s success is built by our 60+ employee nationalities found at offices around the world. We believe that when people feel valued, heard, and empowered, they perform at their best—creating a win for all. Our unique product and dedication to innovation and excellence have raised over $200 million in funding from top investors like Insight Venture Partners, Seed Capital, Dawn Capital, Damgaard Company and Golub Capital.




About the role:

We are looking for a part-time Data Student Assistant to support our Products & Analytics team. In this role, you will contribute to delivering analyses to the commercial organization, build and maintain reports in PowerBI, and document the underlying data structures to ensure transparency and long-term usability.

Our primary tools include SQL, PowerBI, and DBT. If you have an interest in data engineering, you will also have the opportunity to gain exposure to Azure Synapse Analytics, Spark, Python, and Terraform.




The team:

You will join a small, collaborative team consisting of one Senior Data Analyst, one Data Engineer, and one Student Assistant. Our way of working emphasizes pairing or mobbing on tasks, knowledge sharing, and a focus on delivering value-creating insights rather than simply producing output. This provides a solid learning environment while maintaining clear expectations and professional standards.




We operate on a mature data platform, and because our team covers both data engineering and analytics, we have limited external dependencies that impact our delivery. This enables us to work efficiently and take ownership of our solutions from end to end.




Key responsibilities:

Analytics: Assist the data team in ad-hoc analytics requests, preparing reports, and generating insights to support business decisions
PowerBI: Build and manage reports and dashboards in PowerBI, ensuring data is accurately represented and visually appealing. Collaborate with team members to refine visualisations and add features that enhance data storytelling, and maintain existing reports by updating data sources and incorporating user feedback
Project management support: Assist in managing larger data projects by assisting with project planning, tracking progress, and ensuring deadlines are met
Documentation: assist in documentation tasks for the team. This includes documenting processes, data management practices, and other relevant information




What we are looking for:

The role is well-suited for a student pursuing a degree in data science, analytics, economics, computer science, or a related field who is looking to apply academic knowledge in a structured, fast-paced and professional setting. More specifically:

Currently enrolled in a university program, preferably related to Data Science, Business Analytics, or similar.
Good analytical mindset and comfortable working with data
Detail-oriented and structured
High level of abstract reasoning
English language fluency




Employee Benefits:

Private health insurance
Company lunch contribution
Phone and broadband covered by company
Centrally located office – easy access to public transportation
Company events and team activities
Flexible work environment
Gympass Subscription
Calm App Subscription




At Templafy, we’re all about building an inclusive culture where talented people come together to spark creativity in a dynamic, collaborative space. Strong relationships and teamwork are at the core of everything we do, and with our flat structure, everyone’s ideas count regardless of your role or experience.




We’re big on social events that foster connection and build a diverse, empowering community. From celebrating milestones to participating in employee resource groups or eating lunch together, we create space for meaningful team bonding.




Our mission goes beyond just building a product; we’re shaping the future, and we’d love for you to be a part of this epic journey with us.




Templafy is a workplace of belongingness. To us this means that you have a voice, you dare to speak up, and your voice is heard. We focus on offering an environment that allows all employees to feel that they belong regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, gender, gender identity, genetic information, parental or pregnancy status, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, or other status.

Title: Bliv Business Intelligence Intern hos WPP Media
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About WPP Media

WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. With exceptional talent, trusted data and intelligence, and world-class partnerships – all united by our pioneering agentic marketing platform, WPP Open – we help clients navigate change, capture opportunity, and deliver transformational growth. 

WPP Media is WPP's AI-driven media operating unit, bringing together media, data, and partnerships to deliver creative personalisation at scale. Connected through WPP Open and powered by Open Intelligence, clients see exactly where, how, and why their media investment is working.

Er du interesseret i marketing og nysgerrig efter at lære, hvordan data fungerer bag kulisserne? Så er du måske den person, vi leder efter til en praktikplads i vores Business Intelligence-team!

Om os

Vi er eksperter i at udnytte marketingdata og teknologi til at drive vores kunders forretning fremad. Vores team af dygtige data science- og teknologieksperter udvikler banebrydende løsninger som skræddersyede budalgoritmer, dashboards til medieperformance og dynamiske salgsmodeller. Vi integrerer AI, machine learning, data engineering og business intelligence for at levere exceptionelle resultater til nogle af de største brands i Norden.

Vores BI Solutions-team fokuserer på at udvikle og vedligeholde skalerbare, skræddersyede marketing-dashboards og værktøjer. Vi er et dynamisk team, hvor mange specialister er startet som praktikanter og trives i et uformelt miljø med en god portion humor. Vi lægger vægt på teamwork og et stærkt fagligt fællesskab – og du vil være en vigtig del af vores arbejde.

Om rollen

Som praktikant i Business Intelligence vil du spille en central rolle i at støtte BI Solutions-teamet med at levere datadrevne løsninger til nogle af de største brands i Norden. Du vil få praktisk erfaring og kompetencer, der styrker din profil og forbereder dig på en fremtidig fuldtidsstilling. Dine opgaver vil bla. være:


Støtte BI Solutions-teamet i forskellige kundeprojekter
Vedligeholde og opdatere eksisterende dashboards og dataløsninger
Hjælpe med at oprette og administrere datapipelines
Lære at udføre datakvalitetstjek og test
Bidrage til design og udvikling af rapporter i Power BI
Samarbejde med Data Science- og Engineering-teams om dataintegration
Bidrage til forbedring af BI-processer
Få praktisk erfaring med Google-platforme


Hvem er du?

Vi leder efter dig, som:


Er i gang med en videregående uddannelse inden for digital medier, teknologi, marketing, IT eller kommunikation
Har passion for digital medier og lyst til at lære mere
Er ansvarlig, selvstændig og ambitiøs i din tilgang til ny viden
Har en analytisk og datadrevet tilgang til opgaver
Er en teamplayer med gode kommunikationsevner
Har nemt ved at forstå nye systemer og deres sammenhænge
Tænker i smartere og mere effektive arbejdsgange
Har interesse for datavisualisering, modellering og automatisering


Gode Kompetencer At Have


Grundlæggende kendskab til visualiseringsværktøjer (f.eks. Power BI, Tableau)
Erfaring med SQL og Microsoft Excel
Erfaring med Python er et plus
Kendskab til Git er et plus


Lyder det som noget for dig? 

Så send os dit CV og en motiveret ansøgning, hvor du fortæller os om dine erfaringer, kompetencer og hvorfor netop du er den rette til denne praktikplads. Vi anbefaler, at du søger hurtigst muligt, da vi indkalder til samtaler løbende.

Praktikperiode: 17. august – 16. december 2026. Arbejdstid: 37 timer om ugen (med mulighed for fleksibilitet i forhold til dit studie). Bemærk: Praktikken er ulønnet, men vi tilbyder en gratis frokostordning, morgenmad, gratis holdtræning, fredagsbar og mange andre perks. Og så lover vi, at du går herfra med masser af ny erfaring og et kæmpe netværk.

Om WPP Media

WPP Media er WPP's globale mediebureau. I en verden, hvor medier er overalt og i alt, samler vi de bedste platforme, mennesker og partnere for at skabe grænseløse muligheder for vækst. For mere information, besøg wppmedia.com.

Vær ekstraordinær ved at lede kollektivt for at inspirere transformerende kreativitet.
Skab et åbent miljø ved at balancere mennesker og kundeoplevelser ved at dyrke tillid.
Vær optimistisk ved at fremme vækst og udvikling for at mobilisere virksomheden.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Azure Data Engineer
Som Data Engineer hos cVation bygger du dataplatforme, datawarehouses og MLOps løsninger i skyen for vores kunder. Vi bruger teknologier som Databricks, Fabric, Spark, Azure Machine Learning, Data Factory og Azure Synapse. Vi søger profiler med erfaring inden for enten MLOps, datawarehouses eller dataplatforme, som vil bygge innovative dataløsninger.
cVation er et dansk udviklings- og konsulenthus, som bygger avancerede cloudløsninger på Azure med afsæt i markedets hurtigste og mest agile metoder. cVation blev stiftet i 2014, har modtaget tre Børsen Gazeller og vundet Microsofts Partner Award for App Innovation tre år i træk. Vi arbejder dedikeret med agile arbejdsmetoder, DevOps og Test Driven Development. Vi sætter en ære i at levere software af den højeste kvalitet, og tager desuden principperne fra DevOps og software engineering med i vores dataprojekter, hvilket betyder, at tests, Infrastructure as Code og automatisering er i fokus.
Hvilken rolle får du?
Du kommer til at arbejde på et af vores dataprojekter med et projektteam bestående af arkitekter, software udviklere og DevOps specialister. Du vil have fuldt fokus på ét projekt og være tilknyttet ét team ad gangen.
Din rolle bliver at
rådgive om og bygge datawarehouse/dataplatformsløsninger i skyenbidrage til design og implementering af den optimale Azure infrastruktursikre, at alt opsætning og provisionering sker som infrastructure as code, fx. Terraformfuldautomatiseret overvågning og skalering af løsninger
Hvad forventer vi af dig?
Du har en videregående uddannelse i software engineering, human centered AI el lign. fra DTU, ITU eller KU samt erfaring i data engineeringDu har fx. erfaring i at bygge datawarehouses og dataplatforme og har lyst til at arbejde med data engineeringDu har erfaring med - og vil gerne blive klogere på - flere af følgende teknologier: Data Factory, Databricks, Fabric, Spark, Azure Synapse, Data Warehouse, Data platform, Python, Azure DevOps, Powershell, Azure CLI, ARM templates, YAML, Bicep, Terraform, Git og CI/CDDu vil gerne arbejde med de nyeste teknologier i AzureDu vil gerne indgå i et agilt setup, hvor krav og retning hurtigt kan ændres, men hvor medbestemmelse og frihed er i højsædetDu er flydende i skrift og tale på dansk og engelsk, og trives med at have den daglige kontakt med kundenDu har evnen til at omsætte kundebehov til handling
Det vil være en fordel, hvis:Du er vant til at arbejde som konsulent i et udviklingsteam i tæt samarbejde med arkitekter og software udviklere
Som person er du selvstændig, ansvarsbevidst, initiativrig, eksekverende og serviceminded. Du er drevet af at lære nyt og vild med at holde dig ajour med de nyeste metoder og teknologier. Du er engageret og motiveret i alt hvad du foretager dig. Hvis du også er glad for at sparre med dygtige kollegaer og trives med at løfte i flok, vil du passe perfekt ind hos os.
Hvad tilbyder vi?
Du kommer til at indgå i vores voksende team, hvor høj faglighed, innovation og arbejdsglæde er i højsæde. Det høje faglige niveau blandt dine kollegaer giver en unik mulighed for at arbejde med de bedste, og vi tager din faglige udvikling alvorligt. Vi har en flad organisation med et minimum af administration, hvor ledelsens døre altid er åbne, og der er god plads til både bordfodbold, fredagsbar, frokost på kajen og et aktivt liv udenfor arbejdstid. Flere gange om året afholder vi hackathons, hvor vi i teams dykker ned i præcis de emner, som udviklerne finder interessante. En del af vores kultur er også, at vi gerne ser alle komme med input til hvordan vi kan optimere, udvikle og forbedre cVation.
Hvorfor vælge os som din nye arbejdsplads?
Vores fundament er løsninger bygget på Microsoft Azure Cloud. Alle vores medarbejdere mestrer cloud-native udvikling og DevOps. Vi arbejder i projekter og engagerer os hands-on i fælles teams med vores kunder for at sikre kvalitet og effektiv videndeling.
Vi er passionerede omkring at hjælpe vores kunder med at få det maksimale ud af cloudteknologien. For os er cloud ikke blot infrastruktur eller et nyt datacenter i skyen. Teknologien giver virksomheder mulighed for at skabe væsentligt mere værdi og arbejde med agilitet og innovation på en helt ny måde.
Vi har været med fra starten, da cloud blev udviklet og tilgængeligt. Vi tilbyder således vores årelange erfaring og dybe cloud ekspertise samt en værktøjskasse med alle vores best practices i form af accelerationsplatformen CADD. Den dækker over gennemprøvede værktøjer, rammeværk, effektive udviklingsmetoder og færdige automatiseringskomponenter. Alt dette for at sikre, at projekter kommer hurtigt og sikkert fra start.
Du vil blive en del af en virksomhed, som tror på, at alle har betydning, og at det skal være sjovt at gå på arbejde hver dag. Du vil få kollegaer, der er interesseret i at lære og har ønske om at blive de bedste til det, de gør. Vi tilbyder derfor gode muligheder for at udvikle dig.
Interesseret?
Klik på Easy Apply knappen, hvis du vil arbejde med de nyeste teknologier. Hvis du har under 5 års erfaring, bedes du også vedhæfte dine karakterblade i samme fil som dit CV. Vi behandler din ansøgning fortroligt og arrangerer løbende interviews.
Har du spørgsmål til jobbet, eller vil du høre mere om cVation, er du meget velkommen til at kontakte:
Maiken Pelby, Head of People & Culture, T/ 42612828, map@cvation.com
Vi ser frem til at høre fra dig!
Show more

Title: Data Analyst (Danish) | $15/hr Remote
Place: Denmark
Description:
Position: LLM – AI Quality Analyst (Danish)

Type: Short-Term Contract

Location: Remote

Commitment: 20-40 hours/week with 4 hours overlap with PST

Engagement Length: 1 month

Start Date: Immediate




Role Responsibilities

Design multi-turn conversational prompts based on personal context
Evaluate personalized AI responses for relevance, grounding, and helpfulness
Assess correct and incorrect use of personal data in model outputs
Perform side-by-side (SxS) evaluation and ranking of AI responses
Identify grounding errors, poor inferences, and forced personalization
Write clear, structured rationales referencing specific conversation turns
Extract and verify model debug information and data source usage
Maintain strict data hygiene by deleting evaluation conversations




Requirements

Danish fluency (reading and writing) is mandatory, as Danish is the focus language for this project.
Experience in data annotation, AI quality evaluation, content moderation, or related roles is strongly preferred
Strong analytical thinking and attention to detail
Ability to evaluate nuanced and ambiguous AI responses
Comfortable using a primary personal Google account with enabled data sources
BS/BA degree or equivalent experience in a relevant analytical field
Strong written communication and structured feedback skills
Self-motivated and able to work independently in a remote setting
Reliable desktop/laptop with stable internet connection




Application Process

Apply/Easy Apply and check email for application form
Fill Google form
Assessment Link (After shortlisting – to be completed within 24 hours)
Language vetting

Title: Student Analyst (SE Market) with Data Analysis Expertise
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join our Swedish Business Development team and put your analytical skills to work where they truly matter.

As a Student Analyst, you’ll work hands-on with data that supports real commercial decisions—helping identify product and market opportunities by transforming complex datasets into clear, actionable insights. If you’re studying economics, math, engineering, or a similar field and eager to strengthen your data toolkit, we’d love to hear from you.




Drive meaningful change

As a Student Analyst in the Swedish Business Development team, you will play an essential role in driving growth, developing operational procedures, and analyzing new potential products and market opportunities to grow our portfolios.

In this role, your focus is to strengthen our decision-making through structured analysis. You’ll work with data from multiple sources, build clear overviews, and translate findings into insights that help us prioritize the right products and market opportunities for Sweden.

You will experience a high degree of trust and responsibility in your job, making the work both challenging and rewarding.




Get a glimpse of our world

You will be part of a small, dynamic, and ambitious team focused on professional development, individual responsibility, team spirit, and an informal tone. Our team currently consists of three student analysts and six full-time employees.

We value diversity and believe that having varied perspectives drives innovation and growth. That’s why we’re looking for two distinct profiles for these roles to take our team’s impact to the next level.




Are you ready to embark on this journey with us?

You enjoy working in a commercial environment and want to support portfolio and product decisions through structured analysis. You are curious about markets and products—and you see data as a practical tool to help teams make better, more informed decisions rather than as an end in itself.

Are in the second or third year of your bachelor’s degree (e.g. economics, math, engineering, statistics, or a similar field)
Have strong analytical skills and a genuine interest in developing your data capabilities professionally
Maintain solid Microsoft Excel skills; experience with tools such as Power BI, SQL, or Python is a plus but not required
Enjoy working with larger datasets and creating clear, structured overviews
Work in a systematic way and care about accuracy, logic, and reproducibility

Title: Consultant for Data Science Consultancy Team
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Annalect, we help businesses across industries make better decisions — from marketing strategies to business development and sales forecasting. Our approach combines deep industry understanding with advanced, AI-powered analytical methods, enabling us to deliver insights and recommendations that make a real difference for our clients. In Denmark, we are more than 70 specialists based in Copenhagen and Aarhus. As part of Omnicom Media Group, we have over 300 colleagues in Denmark and a global network of more than 5,000 experts with whom we collaborate to create the best possible solutions for our clients.

Consultant for Data Science Consultancy Team

Are you passionate about applying Data Science in real-world scenarios? Do you want to leverage mathematics, statistics, and programming to help our clients make better decisions? Are you driven by data and analysis, and eager to translate insights into actionable recommendations? Join Annalect’s Data Science Consultancy team!

About The Role

We are looking for a new colleague to join Annalect, Omnicom Media Group’s specialist unit for data-driven analytics and decision support systems. Our work includes analyses that quantify the impact of our clients’ marketing activities and provide the foundation for ongoing optimization. Annalect consists of 60 employees, with 20 dedicated to the Data Science Consultancy team. Our team utilizes mathematics, statistics, and econometrics to process large volumes of data, delivering business-oriented decision support. As a Data Science Consultant, you will be involved in the entire project lifecycle for our clients – from data collection and coding, to building and optimizing statistical models in R, and communicating results and recommendations to clients. You will work closely with a project lead and the team, receiving ongoing support and feedback. As you gain experience, you will have the opportunity to conduct analyses independently, manage client communications, and deliver your own projects.

Key Responsibilities


Collect client data and extract data from media systems
Programming and data processing
Structure, code, and interpret data
Develop statistical models in R
Analyze results and prepare recommendations in close collaboration with a project lead
Prepare deliverables for clients in PowerPoint and dashboards
Maintain ongoing dialogue with clients and media agencies throughout the project
Lead your own projects


Your Profile

We Imagine That You


Hold a relevant academic degree (e.g., Cand.Merc. (MAT), Cand.polit. IMM, AEF, or similar)
Have basic experience with statistical models and methodologies
Possess strong analytical skills
Are proficient in R Studio (or other programming languages such as Python, SAS, VBA, SQL and are eager to further develop your skills in R Studio)
Are experienced in Excel and PowerPoint
Are interested in building an understanding of marketing, digital platforms, and the media landscape


What We Offer

We offer a position in an exciting company with many opportunities. You will be part of a strong professional community and work alongside some of the industry’s most talented specialists. We also have a vibrant social environment, including Friday bars, annual parties, company football team, DHL race, and more. Our culture is relaxed, with room for fun and focus for deep work on projects.

Interested?

We look forward to receiving your application and CV as soon as possible. We conduct interviews on an ongoing basis and reserve the right to close the posting once the right candidate has been found.

If you have any questions about the position or the company, please contact Analytics Director Anders Mousten at anders.mousten@omc.com.

Title: Machine Learning Researcher
Place: Copenhagen Metropolitan Area
Description:
Machine Learning Researcher — real feedback cycles!

Do you want to see your models make decisions in live financial markets — not sit in a notebook waiting for a quarterly review? Are you as comfortable thinking about data pipelines and calibration frameworks as you are about model architecture?




A market-leading quantitative trading firm is looking for a Machine Learning Researcher to join their modelling team in Copenhagen. They've been automating financial markets since 2008, and ML sits at the core of how they operate. This isn't a role where you hand off to an engineering team and wait. You'll own the work from data generation through to deployment — and in this environment, that cycle can be measured in hours, not months.




What you'll do

▸ Lead modelling workflow — build and improve the frameworks that turn massive financial datasets into predictive models that move with the markets.

▸ Develop and optimise — work on calibration and benchmarking frameworks, data preparation pipelines, and dataset generation at scale.

▸ Ship novel architectures — prototype and release new predictive models with a particular focus on time-series approaches.

▸ Work with real data, real stakes — your models get validated against live markets. Feedback is immediate and unambiguous.

▸ Collaborate closely — work alongside quant researchers, software developers and traders who all write code and understand the craft.




The team

Eighteen quantitative researchers across six nationalities — thirteen PhDs and five MScs — with expertise spanning statistical analysis, mathematical modelling and machine learning. No red tape, no sales layer between you and your work. Technical discussions are valued, ownership is expected, and the team genuinely enjoys what it does. Successes are celebrated.




What they're looking for

✓ Strong foundations in mathematics and statistics — you think rigorously, not just empirically. ✓ End-to-end ML experience — from data generation and wrangling through model calibration, validation and live monitoring. You've built systems that run in production, not just experiments that ran in a notebook.

✓ Research depth — whether from academia or industry, you can point to work where you designed experiments, validated results, and drew meaningful conclusions from data. Published research is a strong positive signal but not a requirement.

✓ Solid Python skills — data-wrangling, numerical programming, production-quality code.

✓ Computer science fundamentals — you understand what's happening under the hood.

✓ A scientific, inquisitive mindset — you ask why the model works, not just whether it does.

Financial markets knowledge is a plus but not a prerequisite — the team cares more about how you think and build than which domain you come from. If your background is in physical sciences, engineering, or applied mathematics and you've been working on hard prediction problems with real data, we want to hear from you.




Nice to have: If you have experience with time-series forecasting, out-of-core datasets, PyTorch, TensorFlow, XGBoost or CatBoost, C# or C/C++, and a PhD or MSc in engineering, physics, computer science, mathematics or economics that is all awesome!




What they offer




💰 Competitive compensation package

Pension, health insurance and 30 days of vacation
Modern harbourfront office in Copenhagen (Nordhavn)
Hybrid working model - 4 days in the office, 1 day remote
Flat hierarchy — high trust, high autonomy, real freedom to make decisions
Short feedback cycles — from inception to deployment can be hours, not quarters
Strong team culture with company events and trips

Title: Senior Data Analyst (Relocate to Cyprus)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join our Player Care team at Scorewarrior. The Player Care team is at the forefront of providing customer service to our players, as well as safeguarding the community integrity through enforcing Fair & Safe Play, improving moderation efforts, and ensuring operational excellence while at it.




Scorewarrior is a game developer and publisher based in Limassol, Cyprus. The company focuses on the 4X strategy genre, with an ambitious goal to grow its hit game, Total Battle, to become the #1 strategy game in the world, and this role is key to achieving this. Our team consists of 200+ passionate, talented, self-driven, and continually developing professionals who love making high-quality games.




Our product: We are currently working on Total Battle — an MMO 4X strategy game with a deep political system and lots of player interaction. Players take on the role of a commander and need to develop their city, forge alliances, fight against monsters, and conquer other players. We're constantly developing new game mechanics, and the project is continuing to grow.




At the heart of our Player Care department, our mission is to excite our players with exceptional customer care, ensuring they feel valued and supported throughout their journey with us.




What you’ll do

Build and maintain the single "Source of Truth" semantic data layer for the entire Player Care department, enabling non-technical leads to perform ad-hoc analysis.
Design, build, and maintain reliable metrics dashboards tailored to the operational needs of different Support and Trust & Safety teams.
Ensure all operational metrics (CSAT, SLA, etc.) are clearly defined, reliable, and calculated correctly across all reports.
Assist and mentor the support team with SQL query composition and data literacy.
Conduct A/B tests to measure the effect of Support and Trust & Safety initiatives on relevant departmental and broader business metrics.
Analyze the correlation between support quality, bug-related friction, and subsequent player behavior, with a strong focus on VIP cohorts.
Partner with the safety team to analyze data for fraud detection, including social engineering and account sharing.
Perform time-series forecasting for workload volume based on the LiveOps calendar to optimize shift headcount and SLA coverage.
As a future ambition, build and maintain an early warning predictive model for VIP player churn.




What you have

Strong SQL skills and data modeling experience
Experience with BI tools (e.g. Tableau or equivalents)
Proven experience in product or operational analytics
Ability to design analytical events and metrics aligned with product goals
Strong analytical thinking and ability to translate data into actionable insights
Confident knowledge of mathematical statistics and probability theory
Hands-on experience with causal inference and A/B testing
Familiarity and advocacy of AI tooling (ideally Claude)
Patience and willingness to advocate for data literacy and help operational teams learn basic data concepts




Nice to haves

Hands-on experience with ClickHouse and dbt
Experience analyzing LiveOps features, complex game economies, or VIP player behavior, ideally in mid-core or 4x strategy games.
Experience working with Customer Support or Trust & Safety (fraud detection, toxicity)
Experience working with large player bases and complex event-driven systems




Where you’ll work

We value face-to-face communication, and we strongly believe that genuine human interactions move us closer to our ambitious goal. This is part of our culture, and this is why this job offers no remote or hybrid work opportunities.

You will be working in Limassol, Cyprus. If you’re not from around here, fear not! Our dedicated relocation team will provide comprehensive support helping you and your family move to Limassol.




What we offer

The best team in the world!
An excellent remuneration package, including a 13th salary.
Fully-covered relocation to Limassol, Cyprus for you and your family.
A modern and sunny office space close to the sea.
Private health insurance for you and your family members.
A monthly school allowance for your children at pre-school and school-age.
Lunch compensation.
Personal development opportunities paid for by the company: professional and language courses, books, etc.
Bonuses for outstanding results, years worked, getting married, and the birth of a child.
Monthly office parties and epic, annual offsite company birthday and Christmas parties.

Title: Data Analyst, Sourcing – Viby J
Place: Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Driving Digital Transformation Through Insightful Analytics

Are you passionate about bridging the gap between business stakeholders and our developers? As a Business Analyst at STARK GROUP IT, you will play a key role in enabling our Sourcing Analytics team in our digital transformation by analysing data and translating business requirements into tangible work for our developers. You will collaborate with stakeholders across Europe and on many different levels from Process Owners to Architects, ensuring our digitalisation stays on track. If you are ready to make a real impact and help STARK become a truly data-driven organization, we would love to hear from you.

Your Role and Impact

You will work closely with architects, data engineers, BI developers, and sourcing leaders within STARK Group IT and across our seven European business units. Your job is to translate business needs into clear technical specifications, ensure the right data teams are engaged, and guide initiatives from requirement to delivery. You will also support our Athene sourcing projects by analysing and documenting data sources and ensuring they are made available and tested.

In this role you will:


Collaborate with business and technical teams to uncover, challenge, and clarify data requirements while ensuring accurate documentation.
Translate business needs into structured, actionable technical specifications for development and data teams.
Identify and assess relevant data sources in cooperation with stakeholders and data domains and ensuring alignment with enterprise data management practices.
Ensure final solutions meet agreed objectives, manage change requests, and maintain progress across teams.
Facilitate alignment on KPIs and definitions, and ensure they are properly documented in the enterprise data catalog in line with Data Management CoE standards.


You will be successful in this role when you consistently ensure timely delivery and receive strong stakeholder feedback on both the outcome and the collaboration. By acting as a clear bridge between business and developers, you translate business needs into well-defined data requirements and actionable data analysis that create real business value. Your success is driven by strong communication, analytical thinking, and your ability to navigate a collaborative environment.

What You Bring

We are looking for someone who is structured, curious, and confident operating across both business and technical environments. You enjoy turning complexity into clarity and fostering alignment across teams.

You bring:


Strong ability to communicate clearly and influence stakeholders across organisational layers.
Proven experience translating business needs into technical data requirements and guiding datafocused projects.
Ability to evaluate and structure change requests, paired with strong attention to detail and a strategic mindset.
Familiarity with building templates or defining new ways of working, and basic skills in SQL and Databricks.
Minimum 2 years of hands‑on, professional experience in a similar role. This is not an entry‑level position.


You will use a modern data stack that includes Azure Databricks, Power BI, Azure DevOps, Qlik Replicate, Kafka and our enterprise data catalog in Open Metadata. It is also helpful - but not required - if you have some familiarity with BI or Data Management processes, sourcing related domains, agile ways of working, ServiceNow, or Azure components such as Synapse or Data Factory.

Welcome to Group IT

When you join Group IT, you join a collaborative international organisation where technology and business go hand in hand. We are replacing legacy systems and building a modern, modular IT landscape to support STARK Group’s continued growth. You will become part of a community that values knowledge sharing, professionalism, and building solutions that make a clear difference to colleagues across Europe.

Want to know more and apply?

We are happy to answer any questions you may have regarding the role or your opportunities at STARK Group. Please reach out to Kristine Grann at Kristine.grann@stark.dk for more information.

STARK Group 

STARK Group is a growing private equity-owned company with a strong legacy. STARK Group was first established in Denmark in 1896 and has today evolved into a leading European retailer and distributor of heavy building materials with net sales of approx. EUR 9 billion. With a dedicated team of more than 20,000 employees and +1,150 branches and distribution centres across the Nordics, Greenland, Germany, Austria and the UK, we live and breathe to serve professional craftsmen with heavy building materials all the way from the Austrian Alps to Tromsø, close to the Arctic circle and everywhere in between. STARK Group is committed to reach net zero emissions by 2050 across the entire business, is a signatory to the UN Global Compact, and works within fully 2030 validated science-based targets to keep global warming below 1.5°C 

At STARK Group IT, we believe in the richness diversity brings. We invite individuals of all backgrounds to join us in creating an inclusive and welcoming environment. We believe in a workplace where everyone can bring their authentic selves to work. 

Agency Policy 

STARK Group IT appreciates the interest of all candidates. However, we prefer to directly engage with individuals seeking employment opportunities. Therefore, we kindly request recruitment agencies, headhunters, and third-party staffing services to refrain from contacting us regarding this job posting. All applications should be submitted directly by the candidates through our official application process. We value direct communication and look forward to reviewing your application.

HTML:
Driving Digital Transformation Through Insightful Analytics

Are you passionate about bridging the gap between business stakeholders and our developers? As a Business Analyst at STARK GROUP IT, you will play a key role in enabling our Sourcing Analytics team in our digital transformation by analysing data and translating business requirements into tangible work for our developers. You will collaborate with stakeholders across Europe and on many different levels from Process Owners to Architects, ensuring our digitalisation stays on track. If you are ready to make a real impact and help STARK become a truly data-driven organization, we would love to hear from you.

Your Role and Impact

You will work closely with architects, data engineers, BI developers, and sourcing leaders within STARK Group IT and across our seven European business units. Your job is to translate business needs into clear technical specifications, ensure the right data teams are engaged, and guide initiatives from requirement to delivery. You will also support our Athene sourcing projects by analysing and documenting data sources and ensuring they are made available and tested.

In this role you will:

  • Collaborate with business and technical teams to uncover, challenge, and clarify data requirements while ensuring accurate documentation.
  • Translate business needs into structured, actionable technical specifications for development and data teams.
  • Identify and assess relevant data sources in cooperation with stakeholders and data domains and ensuring alignment with enterprise data management practices.
  • Ensure final solutions meet agreed objectives, manage change requests, and maintain progress across teams.
  • Facilitate alignment on KPIs and definitions, and ensure they are properly documented in the enterprise data catalog in line with Data Management CoE standards.

You will be successful in this role when you consistently ensure timely delivery and receive strong stakeholder feedback on both the outcome and the collaboration. By acting as a clear bridge between business and developers, you translate business needs into well-defined data requirements and actionable data analysis that create real business value. Your success is driven by strong communication, analytical thinking, and your ability to navigate a collaborative environment.

What You Bring

We are looking for someone who is structured, curious, and confident operating across both business and technical environments. You enjoy turning complexity into clarity and fostering alignment across teams.

You bring:

  • Strong ability to communicate clearly and influence stakeholders across organisational layers.
  • Proven experience translating business needs into technical data requirements and guiding datafocused projects.
  • Ability to evaluate and structure change requests, paired with strong attention to detail and a strategic mindset.
  • Familiarity with building templates or defining new ways of working, and basic skills in SQL and Databricks.
  • Minimum 2 years of hands‑on, professional experience in a similar role. This is not an entry‑level position.

You will use a modern data stack that includes Azure Databricks, Power BI, Azure DevOps, Qlik Replicate, Kafka and our enterprise data catalog in Open Metadata. It is also helpful - but not required - if you have some familiarity with BI or Data Management processes, sourcing related domains, agile ways of working, ServiceNow, or Azure components such as Synapse or Data Factory.

Welcome to Group IT

When you join Group IT, you join a collaborative international organisation where technology and business go hand in hand. We are replacing legacy systems and building a modern, modular IT landscape to support STARK Group’s continued growth. You will become part of a community that values knowledge sharing, professionalism, and building solutions that make a clear difference to colleagues across Europe.

Title: Data Collector
Place: Denmark
Description:
Global Freelance Opportunity | Gig Workers Needed for AI Data Collection




We are currently recruiting gig-workers across multiple countries to support a global AI training data project.




The task is simple: capture photos of everyday objects at home and upload them to our platform.




No professional photography experience is required.




💰 Compensation

Earn $0.28 per approved image

Typical contributors upload 100–500 images, allowing you to earn $30–$140 per session depending on participation.




📸 What You Will Do

Take clear photos of common objects around you
Upload them with the object name and image details(Dropdowns)
Follow simple submission guidelines




📦 Example Categories

We are collecting images across many real-world product categories, including:

Household Items, Electronics, Clothing, Furniture, Food & Beverage, Tools & Hardware, Toys, Books, Musical Instruments, Baby Products, Medical Items, Cleaning Products, Eyewear, Retail Objects, Signs, Watches, Containers, and more.




🌎 Project Scope

This is a large-scale international project, with each country contributing 4,000 – 16,000 images.

We are looking for hundreds of contributors globally.




👤 Ideal For

Freelancers
Students
Gig workers
Remote workers
Anyone looking for flexible side income




📩 How To Join

Step 1 - Apply by registering on our Gig-Sourcing Platform (Click Here)

Step 2 – Apply for the project “Simple Image Collection - Chester Global”

Step 3 - Communication & Support Channel - https://discord.gg/BkYahnm34

Step 4 – Download data collection mobile app – AIDAC (Available for iOS & Android)

Step 5 – Video for AIDAC usage - https://app.box.com/s/3d8xlbhw32qt5x6sy50wcbk39bgyw7ur

Step 6 – Use the same email ID for data submission on AIDAC

Step 7 – Get started 😊

**Spots are limited per country, so early registration is recommended.

Title: Solution Manager, Data & Analytics
Place: Frederiksberg Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Fiskars Group we are unified and driven by our common purpose: pioneering design to make the everyday extraordinary. Join our team and seize the extraordinary opportunity to influence the everyday!

We’re looking for a Solution Manager, Data & Analytics to help shape the future of our IT systems. In this role, you will be working as part of team of Solution Managers to deliver robust, scalable solutions that support our business. At Fiskars, we value people with a growth mindset so you don’t need to know everything once you start; just bring your curiosity, drive, and a collaborative attitude and we will support your learning and growth.

Your Role


Act as a bridge between business and IT to define Data & Analytics solutions.
Lead projects to deliver end-to-end solutions
Support IT transformation by aligning system design with business needs.
Help define a future-proof Data & Analytics solution roadmap.
Participate in global projects and continuous improvement initiatives.
Maintain solution documentation and guide configuration efforts.
Assist in major incident handling and problem resolution.
Learn, grow, and contribute with a curious and collaborative mindset.



Key Responsibilities


Translate business needs into scalable Data & Analytics solutions.
Drive solution design aligned with best practices and enterprise architecture.
Guide internal/external teams on configuration and development.
Support change management, release cycles, and solution lifecycle.
Ensure solution usability, performance, and security.
Collaborate with architects, Business Process Managers (BPM), and partners to deliver high-quality results.
Identify new opportunities and propose improvements.
Contribute to Data & Analytics processes such as AutoML.



What You Bring


3+ years in Data & Analytics development and delivery.
Strong hands-on experience in solution configuration.
Understanding of global templates and end-to-end implementations.
Experience with tools like Fabric, Azure, Databricks, PowerBI and Jira
Knowledge of Data & Analytics integrations.
Strong communication and collaboration skills in English.
Ability to work in a global, cross-functional environment.
Passion for continuous learning and improving business through Data & Analytics.



Apply now!

Excited about this role? So are we! We are reviewing applications on a continuous basis so please submit your application already today!

Last day of application:

At Fiskars, we value providing benefits that matter to you and cater to our diverse global workforce, no matter where you are in the world. While benefits may vary based on location, here are some of the incredible offerings you can expect when joining our team:

Competitive Compensation: We ensure that our employees are fairly compensated based on industry standards and local market factors.

Health and Wellness: Your well-being matters to us, no matter where you are. We offer various health and well-being focused programs and coverage, adapted to the requirements of each region.

Work-Life Balance: We understand the need for balance in your life. Our paid time off plans and flexible work arrangements and policies are designed to accommodate local customs and personal commitments, enabling you to thrive in both your professional and personal life.

Professional Development: We believe in nurturing talent and supporting professional growth. Through training programs, skill development initiatives and learning resources, we empower our employees to reach their full potential.

Inclusive and Diverse Environment: We value diversity and promote inclusivity. Join a workplace where your unique background and perspectives are celebrated, fostering collaboration and respect across all our markets.

Recognition and Rewards: We believe in celebrating and recognizing the contributions of our employees worldwide. Our recognition programs are designed to acknowledge exceptional performance and achievements, ensuring that your efforts are celebrated and valued.

Vested Interest: Through our Employee Share Purchase Plan (ESPP), you have the opportunity to invest in Fiskars Group and become a proud co-owner, sharing in the success and growth of the company.

Employee Discounts: We want to ensure our team members make each day the extraordinary with use of our products and offer product discounts across our brands.

Please note that specific benefits may vary based on local regulations, market conditions, and other factors. We are committed to providing competitive and meaningful benefits to our employees in every market we operate.

Pioneering design to make the everyday extraordinary

Fiskars Group (FSKRS, Nasdaq Helsinki) is the global home of design-driven brands for indoor and outdoor living. Since 1649, we have designed products of timeless, purposeful, and functional beauty, while driving innovation and sustainable growth. In 2024, Fiskars Group’s global net sales were EUR 1.2 billion and we had close to 7,000 employees. We have two Business Areas (BA), Vita and Fiskars.

BA Vita offers premium and luxury products for the tableware, drinkware, jewelry and interior categories. Its well-known brands include Georg Jensen, Royal Copenhagen, Wedgwood, Moomin Arabia, Iittala and Waterford. In 2024, BA Vita’s reported net sales were EUR 605 million. Already 50% of BA Vita’s net sales comes from direct-to-consumer sales, comprising approximately 500 stores and approximately 60 e-commerce sites.

BA Fiskars consists of the gardening and outdoor categories, in addition to the scissors and creating, as well as cooking categories. The brands include Fiskars and Gerber. In 2024, BA Fiskars’ net sales were EUR 547 million.

Read more: fiskarsgroup.com

Title: Data Scientist (Python & SQL) - Freelance AI Trainer
Place: Denmark
Description:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:


Design original computational data science problems that simulate real-world analytical workflows across industries (telecom, finance, government, e-commerce, healthcare)
Create problems requiring Python programming to solve (using Pandas, Numpy, Scipy, Sklearn, Statsmodels, Matplotlib, Seaborn)
Ensure problems are computationally intensive and cannot be solved manually within reasonable timeframes (days/weeks)
Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains in data processing, statistical analysis, feature engineering, predictive modeling, and insight extraction
Create deterministic problems with reproducible answers: avoid stochastic elements or require fixed random seeds for exact reproducibility
Base problems on real business challenges: customer analytics, risk assessment, fraud detection, forecasting, optimization, and operational efficiency
Design end-to-end problems spanning the complete data science pipeline (data ingestion → cleaning → EDA → modeling → validation → deployment considerations)
Incorporate big data processing scenarios requiring scalable computational approaches
Verify solutions using Python with standard data science libraries and statistical methods
Document problem statements clearly with realistic business contexts and provide verified correct answers


What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for Data Science specialists with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:


5+ years of hands-on data science experience with proven business impact
Portfolio of completed projects and publications showcasing real-world problem-solving
Expert Python programming for data science (pandas, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, statsmodels)
Expert statistical analysis and machine learning - deep understanding of algorithms, methods, and their practical applications
Expert with SQL and database operations for data manipulation and analysis
Experience with GenAI technologies (LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, vector databases)
Understanding of MLOps practices and model deployment workflows
Knowledge of modern frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain)
Strong written English (C1+)


How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $58 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Intern for Model Development
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you curious to learn how models are developed and implemented in the real world? Would you like to be the one to futureproof our processes by applying AI solutions? Are you keen to learn something new, prepared to be surprised and willing to contribute?

About Us

In Group Risk Analytics, we are building the next generation of credit risk models to help the bank and our customers better manage money in times of need. Our team consists of nine people from various countries, co-located in Copenhagen and Vilnius. With diverse backgrounds, we share a common drive to turn data into models that make sense of the world around us. Most of our time is spent coding in Python and, to a lesser extent, SQL, but we maintain close collaboration with stakeholders in finance, impairments, and credit risk management. The work is demanding but also meaningful, with a fun atmosphere—and the opportunity to drive concrete improvements.

About The Role

You will join a dynamic team where your skills or interest in data science, Python, and AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, and CoPilot will be highly valued. You will actively participate in our meetings, contributing to statistical and coding discussions. Beyond this, you will connect with other teams to understand how we collaborate and suggest ways to enhance our projects. This is a fulltime internship of ideally 6 months. We imagine you are a student who can join as part of your studies as an academic intern.

Your key responsibilities:


Participate in team meetings to discuss statistical and coding matters.
Engage with other teams to understand cross-functional collaboration.
Suggest improvements to ongoing projects to optimise processes.
Support the development and implementation of AI and data-driven solutions.


About You

We are looking for someone who is structured, analytical, and ready to face challenges with curiosity and creativity. You should have a combination of technical skills, a collaborative mindset, and good communication abilities to thrive in this role.


Structured and analytical with good problem-solving skills
Able to manage their own workload with great collaboration skills and a proactive attitude
Understanding of data science and familiarity with Python
Knowledge of mainstream AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, and CoPilot
Proficiency in verbal and written English, as our company language is English


We are hoping to hear from candidates who might have skills in areas such as entrepreneurial business engineering, business analytics or data science. You may possess skills in identifying and solving complex business challenges, applying advanced methods like optimisation and machine learning, or transforming data into actionable insights. You might also bring knowledge in developing innovative solutions, bridging data science with business strategy, or managing frameworks for digital business. Additionally, skills in implementing and optimising AI solutions to create value for organisations would be highly relevant.

Stephan Kim, Head of Super AI, says, "Join a dynamic team where curiosity, creativity, and collaboration are key. You’ll contribute to futureproofing processes with AI and data-driven solutions, driven by a collective drive to improve outcomes. Whether you bring experience in data science, Python, or AI tools—or simply a desire to learn—this role offers the chance to grow and deliver measurable results”.

Ready to join us?

Apply now! We process applications continuously, so we encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. If you have any questions about the job, please contact cc, Stephan Kim Jepsen at stje@danskebank.dk.

We look forward to your application and hopefully welcoming you to our team!

About Us

Danske Bank is a Nordic bank with bridges to the world around us. For 150 years, we have supported people and businesses in releasing their potential. A career with us is an opportunity to join a community of 22,000 colleagues in a culture where we are committed to Teaming Up, Owning It and Being Open. Together, we are on a journey to transform Danske Bank into a better bank. For our customers, our employees and the societies around us.

Danske Bank is a Nordic bank with bridges to the world around us. For 150 years, we have supported people and businesses in releasing their potential. A career with us is an opportunity to join a community of 22,000 colleagues in a culture where we are committed to Teaming Up, Owning It and Being Open. Together, we are on a journey to transform Danske Bank into a better bank. For our customers, our employees and the societies around us.

Title: Business/Data Analyst Praktikanter – Dataanalyse, Forecasting eller Backend
Place: Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Vi søger praktikanter – Dataanalyse, Forecasting eller Backend

Business/Data Analyst Praktikanter – Dataanalyse, Forecasting eller Backend

Har du passion for data og vil du arbejde med konkrete problemstillinger i en moderne dataplatform i forbindelse med din praktik? Vi søger nysgerrige og engagerede praktikanter, der vil være med til at skabe indsigt og værdi gennem analyse, forecasting og visualisering i bl.a. Power BI.

Om os

Du bliver en del af Business Insights, som er en enhed i afdelingen People & Analytics hos YouSee. Vi arbejder med dataanalyse, surveyindsigter og visualisering af KPI’er, der dækker mange af Nuudays forretningsområder. Vi understøtter ledelsen og forretningen med databaserede beslutninger og har fokus på både kvalitet og relevans i de indsigter, vi leverer.

Du får en praktikvejleder, som vil støtte dig gennem hele forløbet, herunder dit praktikprojekt, som vi tilpasser til dine interesser og uddannelsesbehov.

Dine opgaver

Som praktikant vil du kunne arbejde med én eller flere af følgende opgaver – afhængigt af din profil og interesser:


Analyse af kunde- og forretningsdata
Udvikling og vedligeholdelse af dashboards og rapporter
Understøttelse af surveyprojekter med fokus på kundeoplevelser (CX)
Forecasting og statistisk modellering
Backend-udvikling og stabilisering af dataløsninger i Azure og Databricks
Udtræk, rensning og strukturering af data
Deltagelse i ad hoc-analyser og præsentationer til stakeholders



Værktøjer du får mulighed for at arbejde med


SQL (inkl. T-SQL og stored procedures)
Python og AI (til dataanalyse, statistik, API-kald m.m.)
Power BI og DAX
Excel og surveyværktøjet Forsta (ConfirmIT)
Azure Data Factory, Databricks og relaterede cloud-løsninger



Vi forestiller os, at du


Er i gang med en relevant videregående uddannelse (f.eks. datalogi, business analytics, IT & økonomi) og skal i praktik
Har lyst til at bruge og udvikle dine kompetencer inden for dataanalyse og visualisering
Er nysgerrig, struktureret og motiveres af at lære nyt
Trives i et teamorienteret miljø og bidrager positivt til samarbejdet
Kan tage ansvar for egne opgaver og følge dem til dørs
Har kendskab til nogle af de nævnte værktøjer – det er ikke et krav, at du mestrer alle



Vi tilbyder


Et stærkt fagligt miljø med fokus på sparring, udvikling og læring
Et stærkt socialt kollegaskab både på og udenfor arbejde. Herunder flere planlagte arrangementer i afdelingen og i hele virksomheden.
Praktisk erfaring med moderne værktøjer som Azure, Databricks, Power BI, Python, AI og Forsta
Reelle opgaver med betydning for forretningen – ikke bare ”øvelser”
Mulighed for at skræddersy dine opgaver efter dine interesser og mål
Fleksibilitet ift. opstart og varighed – dog ser vi gerne minimum 4 måneders praktik
Praktikken er ulønnet



Din ansøgning

Send din ansøgning og CV snarest muligt – vi indkalder løbende til samtale. Praktikken foregår på dansk, så derfor skal ansøgningen også være på dansk.

Du må meget gerne nævne i din ansøgning, hvilke erfaringer og kompetencer du allerede har, samt hvad du brænder for at lære mere om. Det hjælper os med at tilpasse praktikken til dig.

Vi ser frem til at høre fra dig! Har du spørgsmål, er du velkommen til at kontakte Anders Bjerrum på anden@yousee.dk.

Ansøgningsfrist: 31. maj 2026

Startdato: 1. august 2026 eller efter nærmere aftale

Arbejdssted: Sletvej 30, 8310 Tranbjerg J el. Munnkemølle Stræde 6, 5000 Odense

For yderligere information: Anders Bjerrum, mail: anden@yousee.dk

Nuuday – We make sense with technology

Hos Nuuday er vi 3.000 dedikerede medarbejdere, der på tværs af vores seks brands – YouSee, TDC Erhverv, Telmore, Hiper, eesy og Relatel – arbejder målrettet på at sætte retningen for Danmarks digitale fremtid. Vi befinder os midt i en spændende transformation, hvor vi ikke blot revolutionerer vores IT-systemer, men også skaber innovative digitale kundeoplevelser, der sætter nye standarder for, hvordan danskere, virksomheder og samfundet interagerer med teknologi i hverdagen. Hos Nuuday finder du en kultur præget af mangfoldighed, samarbejde og udvikling. Uanset hvor du arbejder, eller hvilket brand du er en del af, er én ting fælles for os alle: en passion for at skabe løsninger, der gør en forskel. Vi sætter en ære i at forstå vores kunder, og vi ser fremtiden som en mulighed for konstant at udvikle og forbedre os. Vi tror på, at et stærkt fællesskab lægger fundamentet til store resultater, og hos os er der altid plads til dem, der tør tænke nyt, tage ansvar og være med til at bane vejen. Vil du være med?

Title: Senior Quantitative Analyst
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
On behalf of my client, I am currently recruiting for a Flexibility Lead in Copenhagen.




This role will be focused on the energy trading domain, so someone who's has real domain knowledge that wants a new challenge would be a great match.




Quantitative development & optimisation

Build and maintain flexibility trading algorithms for renewable, hybrid and BESS assets, maximising revenue across energy, ancillary services, and capacity markets in Denmark (first, then Germany, then expanding to wider Europe.
Design, back-test, implement in code and continuously improve trading strategies for intraday, imbalance, and frequency response markets.
Build revenue stacking models that quantify and optimise the interplay between co-located revenue streams, including hybrid wind/solar-plus-storage configurations.
Design optimisation models in unison with hybrid PPA offerings, ensuring max value for developers and corporates.
Be responsible and accountable for the realized performance of our flexibility portfolio




Overall, we’d expect you’d spend around 70-80% on the quant development side and 20-30% on the commercial side, being mostly involved in key touchpoints in the commercial dialogue. This might change as we grow our portfolio and will be dependent on the needs of the business.




If this sounds like you please apply directly.

Title: Data Scientist
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
We are seeking an experienced Data Scientist with a Master’s degree in Software Engineering, Computer Science, Physics, Chemical Engineering, or a related discipline. The successful candidate will have a minimum of five years of experience and a proven track record in DevOps-oriented environments, utilizing agile software development practices. The role entails developing a Python library and example notebooks to facilitate domain experts in analyzing large timeseries datasets within a Databricks data warehouse.

Main Responsibilities

The Data Scientist will primarily focus on:


Developing and enhancing a Python library for data analysis.
Creating example notebooks for domain experts.
Implementing automated batch calculations to enrich measurement data.
Collaborating closely with the data science team and stakeholders.
Ensuring alignment with user requirements throughout the development process.


Key Requirements


Demonstrated excellence in Python development.
Proficiency in SQL.
Experience with version control systems, particularly Git.
Proficiency in using Jupyter notebooks.
Experience working with timeseries data.
Strong communication and collaboration skills.


Nice to Have


Experience working in Databricks.
Experience in a cloud environment, preferably Azure.
Experience in developing user-facing Python libraries.
Familiarity with Plotly Dash.
Experience with Apache Spark or PySpark.
Knowledge of CI/CD practices for automated testing and deployment.
Product-oriented mindset with a focus on user needs.
Background or interest in chemistry, chemical engineering, or electrochemistry.

Title: Join us to strengthen data quality, reporting and operational insight @NG Nordic Denmark A/S
Place: Nyborg, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
NG Nordic er en førende leverandør af cirkulære løsninger og miljøservices i Norden. Ved at omdanne affald til værdifulde ressourcer og fjerne farlige stoffer fra kredsløbet minimerer vi emissioner, beskytter naturlige økosystemer og øger adgangen til cirkulære materialer.

NG Nordic er en vigtig del af Nordens industri-infrastruktur og genererer en omsætning på 1,3 milliarder euro med et team på omkring 3.400 medarbejdere og 90 anlæg.

Do you want to work close to the business and help strengthen data, reporting, and system development in an operational environment?




We are looking for a Business Intelligence and Analytics Specialist to support the development of local Danish systems and applications while contributing to reporting, data quality, and analytics across IWS. This is a role for someone who enjoys working in the intersection between operations, ERP, data structures, and business intelligence—and who wants to help build scalable and reliable analytics capabilities together with colleagues across Denmark and the Nordics.




In this role, you will support both the local Danish business and the wider IWS Business Intelligence & Analytics setup. You will help ensure that operational data is accurate, usable, and trusted, while also contributing to reporting solutions and improved data governance practices.


About the role


The purpose of the role is to support local Danish systems and applications development in the ERP and production data environment, while contributing to business intelligence, data quality, and reporting activities across IWS. The role strengthens Denmark’s operational data processes while building scalable analytics capabilities under guidance from the IWS Business Intelligence & Analytics team.




The role is approximately split between two main focus areas:


Local systems, ERP, and data process support


You will:





Maintain and further develop local data structures to enable consistent, reliable, and scalable data use across Danish operations


Act as a local ERP superuser and support configuration, testing, and smaller development activities that improve system performance and data integrity


Improve local data quality and compliance to ensure accurate, timely, and trustworthy inputs for operational, regulatory, and financial reporting


Support stable end-to-end data flows by coordinating with operations, IT, and Nordic stakeholders to resolve issues and develop better practices


Help ensure that operational data reliably supports monthly financial processes and other downstream reporting needs



Business intelligence and analytics support


You will also:





Support the development of Power BI reports and datasets to enable transparent and consistent analytics across IWS


Contribute to data modeling, validation, and preparation activities to ensure reliable analytical outputs


Participate in data governance activities to strengthen shared definitions, metadata, and data management practices


Support and develop the use of IWS data structures to enable reliable reporting, analytics, and data-driven decision-making



What you will be working with


You will work in structured and moderately complex workstreams, helping improve data flows, reporting structures, and system reliability. The role supports both operational and analytical decision-making by ensuring validated data and stronger processes.




You will balance local Danish business needs with Nordic standards and practices, and you will handle routine tasks independently while growing into broader responsibilities over time. The role also contributes to the continuous development of local data and system processes as ERP and operational platforms evolve.


Collaboration and stakeholder management


You will work closely with colleagues across Danish operations, S&OP, finance, production, IT, and Nordic BI stakeholders. Success in the role requires strong collaboration skills and the ability to communicate clearly across functions.




You should be comfortable translating technical and data-related topics into language that makes sense to operational and business stakeholders, and you should be able to work constructively with both local and Nordic colleagues in a matrix organisation.


Your profile


We expect that you have a relevant educational background within data, IT, engineering, finance, or a similar field, and that you are motivated by working with data in a business-oriented and operational context.


We expect that you bring:



Experience with Power BI Desktop and Service


Good knowledge of DAX, Power Query (M), SQL, and data modeling


A basic understanding of reporting governance practices


A basic understanding of modern data ecosystems such as Databricks; experience with Synapse, Snowflake, or Fabric is an advantage


A relevant master’s degree or bachelor’s degree in data science, computer science, finance, industrial engineering, or a related field


Power BI certifications are an advantage



It is also important that you:



Have strong stakeholder management and communication skills


Can translate business requirements into technical solutions


Understand how data, reporting, and system improvements create business and financial value


Work in a structured and detail-oriented manner


Are motivated by learning and developing in a role with both local operational and broader analytical exposure



What we offer


We offer an exciting opportunity to join a business where data, systems, and operations are closely connected. You will have the chance to contribute to both local Danish development and broader Nordic analytical standards, while working with engaged stakeholders across functions.




You will be based in our Nyborg office, with the possibility of a hybrid work model. The role is part of a matrix organisation and involves close collaboration with stakeholders across IT, Operations, SCM, Sales, and Finance.


Interested?


If you want to work in a role where you can combine ERP understanding, data quality, and business intelligence with close interaction with the business, we would like to hear from you.




Send us your CV as soon as possible – we review applications on an ongoing basis but within 2-3 weeks we start inviting for interviews.




If you need further information about the position that is not described above, you are more than welcome to contact Senior Manager, Business Intelligence and Analytics - IWS Finance Eero Sääski on eero.saaski@ngnordic.com // +35 8408452485 or Supply Chain Manager, NG Nordic Denmark A/S Susanne Schultz on susanne.schultz@ngnordic.com // +45 24664757 - All within normal working hours.
