Title: Graduate Data Scientist
Place: Vester Hassing, North Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
The Company

Veovo is a market leader in Airport Smart Technology. With a global footprint of customers and capabilities that cover Airport Operations, Revenue management and Passenger Flow. Some of the world’s largest airports rely on Veovo.  Focused on driving airport performance, our solutions enable airports to handle more aircraft and more passengers, with less delays and less queues. Our platforms are built on a modern tech stack, often delivered from the cloud as SaaS and leveraging intelligence to enable our customer to “go brilliantly”.

Our clients span continents, but all share one thing in common~ they’re big names in airport excellence, with even bigger digital goals. With offices in the UK, Poland, Denmark, New Zealand and the US, Veovo offers a world of opportunity

At Veovo we strive to deliver predictable collaborative solutions that are the platform of great airports. At Veovo we strive to deliver predictable collaborative solutions that are the platform of great airports.

The Opportunity

We focus on solutions in the analysis of queue and movement patterns as well as capacity forecasts. Our product, is cloud-based system that collects and analyzes data via proprietary (Bluetooth / WiFi) sensors, as well as third-party data sources such as camera, radar, infrared counters, coils, boarding cards, calendars, etc.

The solution primarily helps airports to forecast passenger appearance, optimize staff resources, reduce queue and travel times, and improve the experience for the passengers. In addition, we also create solutions for road traffic, railway stations, ports, ski resorts and amusement parks, with similar work and end-results.

Are you ready to be part of the team that brings our brilliant solutions "live" around the world?

The size of our installations are growing rapidly, and we are therefore seeking a skilled analytical person, who can help implement our solutions to help airports predict passenger arrival and flow. The implementation covers requirements, design, and calibration of the system. The calibration is carried out in-house via the setup of our filtering engine.

The Specifics


Identify valuable data sources and automate collection processes
Undertake preprocessing of structured and unstructured data
Analyze large amounts of information to discover trends and patterns
Build predictive models and machine-learning algorithms
Combine models through ensemble modeling
Present information using data visualization techniques
Propose solutions and strategies to business challenges

HTML:
The Company

Veovo is a market leader in Airport Smart Technology. With a global footprint of customers and capabilities that cover Airport Operations, Revenue management and Passenger Flow. Some of the world’s largest airports rely on Veovo.  Focused on driving airport performance, our solutions enable airports to handle more aircraft and more passengers, with less delays and less queues. Our platforms are built on a modern tech stack, often delivered from the cloud as SaaS and leveraging intelligence to enable our customer to “go brilliantly”.

Our clients span continents, but all share one thing in common~ they’re big names in airport excellence, with even bigger digital goals. With offices in the UK, Poland, Denmark, New Zealand and the US, Veovo offers a world of opportunity

At Veovo we strive to deliver predictable collaborative solutions that are the platform of great airports. At Veovo we strive to deliver predictable collaborative solutions that are the platform of great airports.

The Opportunity

We focus on solutions in the analysis of queue and movement patterns as well as capacity forecasts. Our product, is cloud-based system that collects and analyzes data via proprietary (Bluetooth / WiFi) sensors, as well as third-party data sources such as camera, radar, infrared counters, coils, boarding cards, calendars, etc.

The solution primarily helps airports to forecast passenger appearance, optimize staff resources, reduce queue and travel times, and improve the experience for the passengers. In addition, we also create solutions for road traffic, railway stations, ports, ski resorts and amusement parks, with similar work and end-results.

Are you ready to be part of the team that brings our brilliant solutions "live" around the world?

The size of our installations are growing rapidly, and we are therefore seeking a skilled analytical person, who can help implement our solutions to help airports predict passenger arrival and flow. The implementation covers requirements, design, and calibration of the system. The calibration is carried out in-house via the setup of our filtering engine.

The Specifics

  • Identify valuable data sources and automate collection processes
  • Undertake preprocessing of structured and unstructured data
  • Analyze large amounts of information to discover trends and patterns
  • Build predictive models and machine-learning algorithms
  • Combine models through ensemble modeling
  • Present information using data visualization techniques
  • Propose solutions and strategies to business challenges
  • Collaborate with engineering and product development teams

What we are looking for

  • Knowledge of R, SQL and Python; familiarity with Scala, Java or C++ is an asset
  • BSc/BA in Computer Science, Engineering or relevant field
  • Analytical mind and business acumen
  • Experience in data mining
  • Problem-solving aptitude
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills

What we offer in return

  • A job in a growing industry.
  • Employment immediately or by agreement.
  • An exciting position in a company with good working conditions with an open, direct and informal tone
  • A company where ideas are accelerated into action, and everyone can play a part in the development.
  • Excellent opportunities for personal and professional development through independent work assignments.
  • Workplace~ Hækken 2, Vester Hassing, 9310 Vodskov
  • 37 hours working week

Title: Quantitative Analyst - Forward Power
Place: Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Are you an experienced Quantitative Analyst who enjoys turning data, models and ideas into real impact?




We’re looking for a Quantitative Analyst to join our Forward Power team and work at the intersection of quantitative modelling and power trading.




In this role, you’ll collaborate closely with traders to develop and improve quantitative models, analytics and decision-support tools across forward power markets. You’ll work hands-on with data and production-grade code, contributing throughout the full lifecycle from idea to live implementation in trading.




Our Forward Power team has a clear ambition to become a leading force in the European power markets, and we’re looking for a quantitative profile who shares that ambition. If you’re eager to push boundaries and join a team of experienced and driven colleagues, this is your chance.




Let’s dive into the details!




Your tasks will include

Work closely with traders to develop and refine quantitative models that support profitable trading strategies
Help build and develop stack models for CWE markets
Continuously seek improvements to workflows, analytics, risk tools, and business processes
Conduct ad hoc quick analysis to support trading, risk, and commercial decision-making
Design, develop, and maintain analytic and operational views used for monitoring strategies and market dynamics for trading
Support the development and maintenance of Python-based models
Contribute directly to advancing and scaling our data infrastructure




What we're looking for

Minimum 2 years of experience as a Quantitative Analyst, preferably within Forward Power or Day-Ahead
Hold a Master within a quantitative field
You have strong quantitative, analytical, and problem-solving skills
You have great Python knowledge and an eagerness to code. Experience with other code languages is considered an advantage
You’re a strong team player with a pragmatic, execution-focused mindset
You’re driven, take ownership, and are willing to challenge the status quo to create impact




What we offer

A young, dynamic, ambitious and fun work environment
Extensive opportunities for personal development and career growth. You will be given responsibility from day one and receive close sparring with skilled colleagues
The opportunity to contribute and make your mark on a young company’s development
A competitive salary package
A fun and social work environment with events such as Friday bars, poker tournaments, summer and Christmas parties etc. throughout the year. We also offer weekly sports activities to boost team spirit, such as our Nitor-running club!
Delicious lunch arrangement with food from Meyers in our own canteen
Brand new office facilities located in the heart of Aarhus, right next to Dokk1 and Letbanen




Expected start date: As soon as possible

Please note that we review applications on a rolling basis and may close the position early if we find the right candidate.







About Nitor Energy

Nitor Energy is a fast-growing energy trading company, that has swiftly transitioned from start-up to scale-up within just a few years. We’re experiencing rapid growth, offering limitless opportunities for learning and development. We’re proud of who we are and excited about what we’re building for the future together. Our employees thrive on challenges and are motivated by the potential that lies ahead.




Our unique culture integrates traders, coders, and analysts into cohesive teams, creating a well-rounded approach to our success. We believe in a balanced work-life dynamic and foster a vibrant, engaging atmosphere where our employees can excel in every aspect.

Title: Studentermedhjælper til analyseafdeling hos Ingeniøren
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Hos Ingeniøren (Teknologiens Mediehus) søger vi en engageret studentermedhjælper, der kan understøtte vores arbejde med analyse, indsigt og optimering af vores kommercielle aktiviteter. Du vil især komme til at arbejde med spørgeskemaundersøgelser, markedsresearch og business intelligence. Det er derfor en fordel, hvis du har flair for excel, er god til data og statistik, og interesserer dig for digitale medier.




Dine opgaver

Du får en central rolle i arbejdet med at skabe indsigt og understøtte beslutninger på tværs af organisationen.

Dine arbejdsopgaver vil bl.a. omfatte:

Udvikling, opsætning og oversættelse af spørgeskemaer
Dataanalyse og udarbejdelse af tabeller og grafikker i Excel og Looker Studio
Udarbejdelse af præsentationer i PowerPoint
Research og indsamling af data om kunder og brancher
Strukturering og analyse af data til Business Intelligence rapporter
Evt arbejde med SQL, JavaScript




Rollen giver mulighed for at arbejde med reelle problemstillinger og få indblik i, hvordan data og analyser bruges i praksis.




Om dig

Vi forestiller os, at du:

Er studerende på en relevant videregående uddannelse (fx økonomi, business, data, samfundsfag eller lignende) på bacheloren
Har stærke analytiske evner og sans for detaljer
Er komfortabel med at arbejde med dataanalyse og har interesse for markedsanalyse
Kan arbejde selvstændigt og struktureret




Vi tilbyder

Hos Ingeniøren bliver du en del af en dynamisk virksomhed i en mediebranche, der er under konstant udvikling.




Herudover tilbyder vi en arbejdsplads med:

Et spændende studiejob med stor læring og ansvar
Mulighed for at arbejde med analyse i praksis
Et uformelt og professionelt arbejdsmiljø med dygtige og sjove kolleger
Fleksibilitet i forhold til dit studie – særligt under eksamensperioder
Gode muligheder for menneskelig og faglig udvikling, personlig sparring og oplæring fra specialister og ledere i Mediehuset
Mulighed for med tiden at blive ansvarlig for egne projekter, der matcher dine kvalifikationer og interesser
Mangfoldig medarbejderstab, hvor dine kolleger både tæller journalister, ingeniører, cand.merc.er og mange flere mv.
Frokostordning, fredagsbar og gode arbejdsforhold




Praktisk

Arbejdstid: Ca. 10-15 timer om ugen
Teknologiens mediehus har adresse på Gammel Mønt 3A, 1117 København K




Hvad gør du nu?

Hvis ovenstående har vakt din interesse, så send din ansøgning hurtigst muligt via linket i stillingsannoncen og vedhæft dit CV. Vi afholder løbende samtaler, så søg allerede i dag.




Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte analysechef Dennis Roi Hansen på drh@ing.dk eller +45 3326 5376.

Title: MES Specialist
Place: Kalundborg, Region Zealand, Denmark
Description:
MES Engineer (PAS-X) — Denmark — Long-term project!




Our client in the life sciences industry is looking for an experienced MES Engineer with PAS-X expertise to join an ongoing manufacturing execution system programme at a large-scale production site in Denmark.




Location: Kalundborg, Denmark

Contract: Freelance, 12 months with possibility to extend

Start: May 2026




The Role

You will work as the internal PAS-X expert alongside a capable in-house team, reviewing design documents, raising comments, and ensuring automation requirements are met across active MES projects.
Day-to-day scope includes FAT and recipe FAT support, workflow alignment across projects, coordination with engineering and process stakeholders, and developing and delivering operator training.
This is a review and coordination-focused role with daily interface across project and engineering teams. Five days per week on site.




What You Need

2-5 years of hands-on PAS-X experience in a regulated manufacturing environment
Experience across MES project phases including design review, FAT, recipe FAT, and workflow alignment
Ability to work collaboratively alongside strong internal resources
Experience developing or delivering operator training is an advantage
Fluent in English




Does this sound like the right challenge for you or someone you know? Do not hesitate to reach out as interviews are being scheduled on an ongoing basis.

Title: Data Engineer | Life Sciences
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description
Who we are
ADC is a European data and AI consultancy with offices in the Netherlands, Sweden, and Denmark. We help organisations turn data and AI into real impact, leading a mission to accelerate the responsible use of Data and AI. We partner with clients to achieve their goals through data-driven solutions across multiple industries: financial services, life sciences, healthcare, the public sector, retail, and transportation.
Our collective brings together the full data value chain under one roof:

Data Strategy: Turning buzzwords into clear, business‑aligned roadmaps.
Data Engineering: Building secure, scalable, future‑proof data foundations.
Analytics & Data Science: Creating AI‑powered tools and models that actually get used.
Design & Visualisation: With our in‑house studio CLEVER°FRANKE, turning complex data into experiences people enjoy using.

We're leveraging our expertise in data engineering, cloud architecture, and modern data platforms to help Life Science organisations build the data foundations they need to accelerate research, innovation, and patient impact.
Our Life Sciences team is growing, and we are looking for a Data Engineer who is curious, technically driven, and eager to grow with us. As a Data Engineer, you will design and build scalable data pipelines that turn complex, high-volume datasets: from omics and imaging to clinical and experimental data into reliable, production-ready foundations for analytics and AI.
You'll be part of a true collective, working alongside bioinformaticians, data scientists, AI engineers, and client stakeholders to deliver impact-driven solutions from early discovery through to production deployment.
Where you’ll make an impact
Data Pipeline Development & Engineering

Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines for Life Science use cases such as omics, imaging, experimental, and clinical data.
Work with complex, high-volume, and heterogeneous datasets, ensuring data quality, traceability, and reproducibility.
Develop data solutions on cloud platforms (AWS and/or Azure) that follow modern engineering and security best practices.

Technical Delivery & Tooling

Contribute to and implement solutions using Databricks and modern data engineering frameworks.
Support projects end-to-end, from early discovery and design through to production deployment.

Collaboration & Collective Development

Collaborate closely with bioinformaticians, data scientists, AI engineers, and client stakeholders to co-create solutions that work in practice.
Share knowledge internally and contribute to ADC's thought leadership in Life Sciences data and AI.

What You Bring
At ADC, we’re not just consultants: we’re leading experts in Data & AI using our expertise in the Life Sciences industry. What unites us is a shared way of working: breaking down complex challenges to changehow organisations operate.
For This Role, That Means

4+ years of experience in Life Sciences industry as a Data Engineer, ideally gained in consulting or project-based work
A solid foundation in AWS/Azure, Databricks, Python, data modelling, testing and monitoring in production environments
Hands-on experience developing data pipelines for omics data, imaging data, or other scientific datasets
A quantitative academic background: Bioinformatics or similar fields.
The ability to communicate with anyone in the room, breaking down complex ideas for technical and non-technical audiences
A proactive and entrepreneurial mindset: you’re eager to solve problems, innovate, and drive change
A collaborative spirit: You believe the best solutions come from diverse perspectives and supporting each other to raise the bar.
Ability to work at the intersection of science, engineering, and business
Fluency in English.

What’s In It For You
At ADC, you’re part of the collective from day one. We want you to grow, experiment, and shape your own path while working on projects that matter. That means:

Impact you can point to: You’ll see your work in action and our projects change how organisations make decisions.
Real responsibility, real support: You’ll work alongside experienced experts from the start, learning by doing with mentorship and feedback built in.
The freedom to try new things: Spot a better way to build something? A smarter process? We want people who challenge the status quo.
Collective Week: Once a year, we go on a workation with the whole ADC team. We work on pro bono projects, explore new ideas, and make the kind of memories you’ll want to talk about.
Ownership opportunities: We offer a competitive salary and bonus structure, plus the opportunity to invest in the company and become a co-owner once specific conditions are met. We mean it when we say collective.
A vibrant culture: Friday breakfast, sports, social events, and much more: a culture you’ll notice from day one.

What To Do Next
If you believe in building with heart, thrive on collaboration, and want to grow with a team that challenges and supports you, we want to meet you!
If this sounds like your next challenge, apply through the link below.
ADC is an equal-opportunity employer. We value diversity and promote equality to shape a better future. No characteristics are discriminated against, as indicated in Directive 2000/78/EC. We encourage and welcome all applications. Adjustments to support your application can be discussed as needed.

Title: Data Annotator
Place: Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Innodata (NASDAQ: INOD) is a leading data engineering company. With more than 2,000 customers and operations in 13 cities around the world, we are an AI technology solutions provider-of-choice for 4 out of 5 of the world’s biggest technology companies, as well as leading companies across financial services, insurance, technology, law, and medicine.

By combining advanced machine learning and artificial intelligence (ML/AI) technologies, a global workforce of subject matter experts, and a high-security infrastructure, we’re helping usher in the promise of AI. Innodata offers a powerful combination of both digital data solutions and easy-to-use, high-quality platforms.




Our global workforce includes over 5,000 employees in the United States, Canada, United Kingdom, the Philippines, India, Sri Lanka, Israel and Germany.




About the Role




Job Title: Data Annotator

Employment Type: Freelance / Project-Based

Location: Remote (Work From Home)

Work Hours: Flexible; minimum commitment of 4–5 hours per day

Eligibility: Open to candidates with a Bachelor’s degree







We are seeking Data Annotator to support a data annotation and linguistic quality project aimed at training Large Language Models (LLMs). This is a freelance, remote opportunity that offers flexible working hours.

The role involves understanding cultural and linguistic nuances, reviewing and annotating data, and following detailed project-specific guidelines to enhance AI performance and accuracy.




Key Responsibilities




Perform data annotation, and text evaluation in accordance with project-specific guidelines.
Ensure high-quality, accurate, and contextually relevant annotations to support Large Language Model (LLM) training.
Review, validate, and refine AI-generated content for grammatical, semantic, and contextual accuracy.
Maintain consistency and quality across all deliverables while adhering to established project standards and timelines.
Collaborate effectively with the project team to provide feedback and contribute to continuous improvement.
Maintain strict confidentiality and comply with all project deadlines and quality assurance protocols.




Required Skills & Qualifications




Bachelor’s degree in mandatory.
Good proficiency in the English language
Prior experience in data annotation, linguistic evaluation, or AI training projects.




What Makes This Role Exciting




.Fully remote opportunity, allowing you to work comfortably from home.
Flexible working hours (4–5 hours per day) to suit your schedule.
The chance to contribute to cutting-edge AI and Large Language Model (LLM) training initiatives, shaping the future of language technology.




Note:

For this role, you are required to complete the English assessment within two (2) days from the date it is assigned to you.

HTML:
Innodata (NASDAQ: INOD) is a leading data engineering company. With more than 2,000 customers and operations in 13 cities around the world, we are an AI technology solutions provider-of-choice for 4 out of 5 of the world’s biggest technology companies, as well as leading companies across financial services, insurance, technology, law, and medicine.

By combining advanced machine learning and artificial intelligence (ML/AI) technologies, a global workforce of subject matter experts, and a high-security infrastructure, we’re helping usher in the promise of AI. Innodata offers a powerful combination of both digital data solutions and easy-to-use, high-quality platforms.


Our global workforce includes over 5,000 employees in the United States, Canada, United Kingdom, the Philippines, India, Sri Lanka, Israel and Germany.


About the Role


Job Title: Data Annotator

Employment Type: Freelance / Project-Based

Location: Remote (Work From Home)

Work Hours: Flexible; minimum commitment of 4–5 hours per day

Eligibility: Open to candidates with a Bachelor’s degree



We are seeking Data Annotator to support a data annotation and linguistic quality project aimed at training Large Language Models (LLMs). This is a freelance, remote opportunity that offers flexible working hours.

The role involves understanding cultural and linguistic nuances, reviewing and annotating data, and following detailed project-specific guidelines to enhance AI performance and accuracy.


Key Responsibilities


  • Perform data annotation, and text evaluation in accordance with project-specific guidelines.
  • Ensure high-quality, accurate, and contextually relevant annotations to support Large Language Model (LLM) training.
  • Review, validate, and refine AI-generated content for grammatical, semantic, and contextual accuracy.
  • Maintain consistency and quality across all deliverables while adhering to established project standards and timelines.
  • Collaborate effectively with the project team to provide feedback and contribute to continuous improvement.
  • Maintain strict confidentiality and comply with all project deadlines and quality assurance protocols.


Required Skills & Qualifications


  • Bachelor’s degree in mandatory.
  • Good proficiency in the English language
  • Prior experience in data annotation, linguistic evaluation, or AI training projects.


What Makes This Role Exciting


  • .Fully remote opportunity, allowing you to work comfortably from home.
  • Flexible working hours (4–5 hours per day) to suit your schedule.
  • The chance to contribute to cutting-edge AI and Large Language Model (LLM) training initiatives, shaping the future of language technology.


Note:

For this role, you are required to complete the English assessment within two (2) days from the date it is assigned to you.

Title: Business Insights Analyst
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Business Insights Analyst, North & Central Europe

Do you enjoy turning complex data into clear business value? You could be our next Business Insights Analyst, helping shape commercial decisions across North & Central Europe through strong analytics and CRM expertise.

Your new role

As Business Insights Analyst, you will combine business analytics with regional CRM system ownership to ensure high-quality data, stable system operations and actionable insights. Reporting to the Regional Business Insights Lead, you will play a key role in supporting Commercial and Medical teams across the region.

You will own and optimize CRM and related systems, contribute to a new CRM implementation in close collaboration with global functions, and translate data into insights that drive decision-making. The role offers a strong mix of system ownership, stakeholder engagement and analytical work, giving you broad exposure across both affiliate and global levels.

Your future team

Business Insights, North & Central Europe is a collaborative and stakeholder-focused function supporting regional performance and decision-making. You will work closely with colleagues across Commercial, Medical, affiliates and global teams, as well as external consultants.

The team values curiosity, strong collaboration and a practical approach to problem-solving. You will engage regularly with global CRM communities and regional stakeholders, contributing to shared solutions and continuous improvement.

The position is based in Valby, Denmark. This role is primarily office-based, with the option of working from home up to 2 days per week.

What You Bring To The Team

You bring solid experience within business analytics and systems, combined with a structured and proactive mindset. You are comfortable working across multiple stakeholders and enjoy translating data into meaningful insights.

You likely bring:


A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, Statistics or a related field
5–8 years of experience in Business Analysis, Business Intelligence, Sales Operations or Commercial IT (pharma experience is an advantage)
Strong CRM experience (Veeva preferred) and experience with BI tools such as Power BI, Qlik or Tableau
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills



As a person, you are self-driven and take initiative, with strong project management and problem-solving skills. You communicate clearly, collaborate effectively across functions, and enjoy engaging with stakeholders at both regional and global levels.

Our promise to you

Lundbeck offers an inspiring workplace and innovative culture, where our curiosity, accountability and adaptability enable us to transform lives. We want to go faster and further on addressing the big unmet needs of people living with brain disorders. We offer rewarding careers with a mix of exciting tasks and development opportunities that are balanced with initiatives focused on your well-being.

We need every brain in the game, and at Lundbeck, we are committed to building a workforce that is as diverse as the people we serve. Read more about our commitment at www.lundbeck.com/global/about-us/our-commitment/diversity-and-inclusion

Apply now

Can you see yourself in this role? We want to hear from you. Does this sound like your dream job, but you’re not sure if you meet all the requirements? We still want to hear from you!

Upload your CV and include a few lines about your motivation for applying. We ask you not to include a photo or personal information that does not relate to your professional experience.

Learn more about us at www.lundbeck.com, LinkedIn or Instagram (@h_lundbeck).

#EveryBrainInTheGame

This job ad is intended for individuals seeking a career opportunity with Lundbeck. We engage with recruitment and search firms where needed on the basis of a written agreement, and we do not accept unsolicited requests of any kind. If you work as a recruitment consultant, you are kindly instructed to refrain from contacting Lundbeck or the hiring manager directly with suggested candidates. If you violate this policy, you do so at your own risk and for your own account, and Lundbeck will not assume any liability nor pay any associated fees resulting from such violation.

Title: Mødebooker
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Mødebooker → Salgskonsulent

 

Er du fuld af gå på mod og klar til at bygge en karriere i salg? 

Så er det her ikke bare et job. Det er din træningsplads til at blive vores næste Salgskonsulent. 

 

☕ Hvem er vi? 




Siden 2013 har Coffee by Storm hjulpet virksomheder med at løfte deres kaffeoplevelse.

Vi leverer komplette kaffeløsninger til erhverv - fra speciality kaffe og professionelle maskiner til service i topklasse.  

Vi arbejder blandt andet med brands som La Marzocco, Eversys og Victoria Arduino. 

Vi er et lille, ambitiøst team hvor der bliver fejret wins, drukket masser af god kaffe og hvor vi altid hjælper hinanden med at blive bedre.  

 

🚀 Hvorfor skal du søge?

 

Fordi du elsker at tale i telefon med nye mennesker og på sigt gerne vil lære hele salgsprocessen fra A-Å. Sætter du pris på en rigtig god kop kaffe, vil det være et ekstra plus 😊

 

📞 Hvad skal du lave?




Kontakte virksomheder i hele Danmark via outbound opkald, LinkedIn og mail 
Starte dialogen med nye potentielle kunder og skabe interesse for Coffee by Storm
Booke møder til vores salgsteam 
Kvalificere leads og opbygge pipeline  




Det er her, du lærer fundamentet og bliver trænet i: 




Opsøgende salg via telefon, LinkedIn og mail 
Behovsafdækning  
Hvordan man skaber interesse og bygger relationer  
Hvordan man fører en dialog hele vejen til en aftale  




📈 Udvikling?

 

Du kommer til at:

deltage i salgsmøder og være med på kundebesøg
være med til kaffesmagninger og arbejde tæt sammen med erfarne sælgere




Når du er klar, får du muligheden for at tage næste skridt til Salgskonsulent. 




👀 Hvem leder vi efter?




Du behøver ikke en masse erfaring, men du skal have dette mindset: 

Du motiveres af at skabe resultater  
Du tager initiativ og ansvar  
Du er god med nye mennesker  
Du tør tage telefonen igen og igen  
Du taler og skriver flydende dansk og engelsk  
Du har måske lidt erfaring fra detail, hospitality, service eller anden kundeorienteret rolle, men det er ikke et krav  




💼 Hvad tilbyder vi? 




Fast løn + provision  
Grundig oplæring i salg, kaffe og noget af det bedste kaffeudstyr  
Tydelig karrierevej til Sales Consultant  
Et ambitiøst team med stærkt sammenhold  
Computer og telefon 
Og selvfølgelig… rigtig god kaffe hver dag 




Du arbejder fra vores kontor i Herlev, men dine samtaler rækker ud til virksomheder i hele Danmark. 

 

📩 Er du klar til at komme i gang? 




Send din ansøgning og CV til vores HR Manager Anita Højberg Udsen på job@coffeebystorm.dk - vi gennemgår ansøgninger løbende.   

Ansøgningsfristen er den 24. april 2026.

Title: Souschef - Fuldtid - Herning
Place: Herning, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Introduktion

Kan du motivere branchens bedste kolleger og bidrage til, at butikken altid står snorlige og salgsklar? Så er du måske vores nye souschef.

Hos Lidl bliver du en del af Europas største dagligvarekæde med 150+ butikker i Danmark. Sammen er vi drevet af et fælles mål om at blive danskernes førstevalg, og på #teamlidl kommer du med på en succesfuld rejse – på et hold med høje ambitioner til fælles. Vi har brug for stærke ledere, der giver vores medarbejdere retning og overskud, så vi sammen skaber rammerne for landets bedste indkøbsoplevelse.

Som souschef tager du aktiv del i det daglige arbejde i butikken, og får medansvar for butikkens drift og resultater. Hos os får du ansvar lige fra første arbejdsdag og bliver samtidig en del af et ambitiøst karriereudviklingsforløb. Som Europas største dagligvarekæde har vi nemlig høje ambitioner til fælles – i verden og i hverdagen, med grænseløse muligheder og holdånd i en klasse for sig.

Som souschef i Lidl får du en ansvarsfuld rolle med spændende udfordringer og en konkurrencedygtig lønpakke. Når du starter, klæder vi dig på gennem et grundigt oplæringsforløb i vores koncepter, så du har forudsætningerne for at opnå succes i din nye stilling.

Om jobbet

Som souschef er du med til at drive butikken og lede medarbejderne. Du har medansvar for, at butikken lever op til kundernes forventninger, så de bliver ved med at handle hos os. Ud over at bestille varer, fylde op og ekspedere kunderne ved kassen, får du også følgende opgaver, som du løser i tæt samarbejde med butikschefen:


Planlægning af ressourcer og prioritering af arbejdsopgaver
Oplæring af medarbejdere, så de efterlever Lidls værdier og butikskoncepter
Ledelse af medarbejdere så de lykkes i deres job
Sørge for, at vores butikskoncepter overholdes
Ansvar for butikken når butikschefen ikke er til stede


Om dig

Du er en empatisk og stærk ledertype, der har nemt ved motivere dine medarbejdere. Du bliver respekteret for din faglighed, og du sætter en ære i at tage del i arbejdsopgaverne i den daglige drift, og være med til at sikre, at butikken altid står snorlige og salgsklar.

Du er godt rustet til jobbet, hvis du:


Har brancheerfaring og gerne erfaring fra lignende stilling
Har gennemført et lederkursus eller er motiveret for at udvikle dig som leder
Har en venlig og professionel fremtoning
Inspirerer dine medarbejdere til at følge dit gode eksempel
Har overblik og fokus på detaljen
Ser butikken som et team og ikke er bleg for at give en hånd med, når en kollega har brug for hjælp
Har god forståelse for arbejdsprocesser, procedurer og koncepter


Vi tilbyder

Som souschef i Lidl får du:


En udfordrende rolle, hvor tingene går stærkt
Verdensklasse sammenhold
En konkurrencedygtig lønpakke på 26.000 – 32.500 kr. brutto om måneden
Et udbetalt tillæg i henhold til overenskomsten, når du arbejder på skæve tidspunkter - fx aftener, weekender og helligdage
Løn og vilkår i henhold til gældende overenskomst med HK
En grundig oplæring i vores arbejdsgange og koncepter
Adgang til skræddersyede kurser, der udvikler dig både personligt og fagligt
Mulighed for sundhedsordning
Rabatordning hos flere udbydere af rejser, shopping, kultur, fitness mm.


Vil du med på #teamlidl?

Sådan søger du stillingen:


Klik på ”Søg nu”
Udfyld formularen
Vedhæft dit motiverede CV


Vi rekrutterer løbende, så send dit motiverede CV hurtigst muligt. Da butikken er åben alle ugens dage, vil der forekomme dag-, aften- og weekendarbejde.

Vores rekrutteringsproces består af en telefonsamtale og en fysisk samtale i butikken.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen eller ansøgningsprocessen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Recruiting på jobilidlvest@lidl.dk eller tlf. 7635 0535, mandag – torsdag 8.00 – 15.00/ fredag 08.00 – 14.00.

Vi ser frem til at modtage dit CV!

Title: Data Student Assistant
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About us:

Templafy is the leading AI-powered document generation platform for companies with a need to create accurate, compliant, and on-brand documents with maximum efficiency and ease. Accessible directly from applications like Microsoft Office, Google Workspace and Salesforce, Templafy is trusted by more than 800 industry leading enterprises, including KPMG, Adobe, and PWC. Our platform simplifies document workflows and eliminates the complexity and risk associated with the content creation process.




Founded in Copenhagen, Denmark, in 2014, Templafy’s success is built by our 60+ employee nationalities found at offices around the world. We believe that when people feel valued, heard, and empowered, they perform at their best—creating a win for all. Our unique product and dedication to innovation and excellence have raised over $200 million in funding from top investors like Insight Venture Partners, Seed Capital, Dawn Capital, Damgaard Company and Golub Capital.




About the role:

We are looking for a part-time Data Student Assistant to support our Products & Analytics team. In this role, you will contribute to delivering analyses to the commercial organization, build and maintain reports in PowerBI, and document the underlying data structures to ensure transparency and long-term usability.

Our primary tools include SQL, PowerBI, and DBT. If you have an interest in data engineering, you will also have the opportunity to gain exposure to Azure Synapse Analytics, Spark, Python, and Terraform.




The team:

You will join a small, collaborative team consisting of one Senior Data Analyst, one Data Engineer, and one Student Assistant. Our way of working emphasizes pairing or mobbing on tasks, knowledge sharing, and a focus on delivering value-creating insights rather than simply producing output. This provides a solid learning environment while maintaining clear expectations and professional standards.




We operate on a mature data platform, and because our team covers both data engineering and analytics, we have limited external dependencies that impact our delivery. This enables us to work efficiently and take ownership of our solutions from end to end.




Key responsibilities:

Analytics: Assist the data team in ad-hoc analytics requests, preparing reports, and generating insights to support business decisions
PowerBI: Build and manage reports and dashboards in PowerBI, ensuring data is accurately represented and visually appealing. Collaborate with team members to refine visualisations and add features that enhance data storytelling, and maintain existing reports by updating data sources and incorporating user feedback
Project management support: Assist in managing larger data projects by assisting with project planning, tracking progress, and ensuring deadlines are met
Documentation: assist in documentation tasks for the team. This includes documenting processes, data management practices, and other relevant information




What we are looking for:

The role is well-suited for a student pursuing a degree in data science, analytics, economics, computer science, or a related field who is looking to apply academic knowledge in a structured, fast-paced and professional setting. More specifically:

Currently enrolled in a university program, preferably related to Data Science, Business Analytics, or similar.
Good analytical mindset and comfortable working with data
Detail-oriented and structured
High level of abstract reasoning
English language fluency




Employee Benefits:

Private health insurance
Company lunch contribution
Phone and broadband covered by company
Centrally located office – easy access to public transportation
Company events and team activities
Flexible work environment
Gympass Subscription
Calm App Subscription




At Templafy, we’re all about building an inclusive culture where talented people come together to spark creativity in a dynamic, collaborative space. Strong relationships and teamwork are at the core of everything we do, and with our flat structure, everyone’s ideas count regardless of your role or experience.




We’re big on social events that foster connection and build a diverse, empowering community. From celebrating milestones to participating in employee resource groups or eating lunch together, we create space for meaningful team bonding.




Our mission goes beyond just building a product; we’re shaping the future, and we’d love for you to be a part of this epic journey with us.




Templafy is a workplace of belongingness. To us this means that you have a voice, you dare to speak up, and your voice is heard. We focus on offering an environment that allows all employees to feel that they belong regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, gender, gender identity, genetic information, parental or pregnancy status, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, or other status.

Title: Administrativ sikkerhedskoordinator - compliance, struktur og faktureringsgrundlag
Place: Søborg, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Vil du arbejde i krydsfeltet mellem sikkerhed, administration og drift, hvor detaljen gør forskellen, og hvor dit overblik er afgørende for, at tingene hænger sammen? Her får du en nyoprettet rolle med ansvar for at sikre, at godkendelser, dokumentation og fakturagrundlag spiller fra første gang.

Når struktur bliver en forudsætning for fremdrift

Du bliver omdrejningspunktet for sikkerhedsgodkendelser og administrativ kvalitet på nogle af vores mest krævende rammeaftaler. Opgaverne kræver både overblik og vedholdenhed, fordi du følger processer helt til dørs og sikrer, at intet falder mellem to stole.

Du kommer blandt andet til at:


Sikre og styre processen for sikkerhedsgodkendelser af medarbejdere og underleverandører
Følge op på godkendelser og sikre korrekt prioritering og fremdrift i sagerne Kvalitetssikre fakturagrundlag, herunder EAN, PO-numre, kontaktdata og betalingsbetingelser
Sikre rettidig og korrekt fakturering i henhold til rammeaftaler
Oprette sager, vedligeholde kontaktlister og sikre opdaterede systemer


Derudover får du ansvar for dokumenthåndtering og adgangsstyring, hvor du sikrer, at dokumentation er korrekt placeret, fuldstændig og tilgængelig for de rette personer.

Du skaber ro i kompleksitet og kvalitet i detaljen

Du trives med ansvar og arbejder naturligt struktureret. Opgaver med mange gentagelser og høje krav til præcision motiverer dig, fordi du ved, at kvalitet i detaljen er afgørende for helheden. Samtidig formår du at holde hovedet koldt, når flere processer kører parallelt.

Andre beskriver dig som vedholdende og du slipper ikke en opgave, før den er løst korrekt. Samarbejde falder dig naturligt, og du forstår værdien af, at være en stærk støttefunktion for både drift og ledelse.

Fagligt forestiller vi os, at du:


Har min. 1 års erfaring fra en administrativ rolle med fokus på struktur og datakvalitet, som evt. kan komme via studiejob
Har arbejdet med fakturering, ordreoprettelse eller lignende processer
Er rutineret bruger af Excel og administrative systemer
Har erfaring med at håndtere mange samtidige opgaver under stort tidspres
Det er en fordel, hvis du har kendskab til compliance, godkendelsesprocesser eller projektorganisationer, men det er ikke et krav


Hvem er vi?

Du bliver en del af en organisation, hvor du refererer til CCO og arbejder tæt sammen med både drift, økonomi og ledelse. Beslutningsvejene er korte, og der er plads til at tage ansvar fra dag ét.

Du bliver en del af en virksomhed, hvor vi arbejder med komplekse rammeaftaler og høje krav til kvalitet og dokumentation. Rollen er central for, at vores projekter kan gennemføres effektivt og korrekt.

Interesseret?

Kan du se dig selv i ovenstående, så send hurtigst muligt din ansøgning ved blot at svare på et par enkelte spørgsmål.

Vi gennemgår ansøgninger og holder samtaler løbende. Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte CPO Heidi Wassini på hewa@vogw.dk.

Ansøgningsfrist: 27. april 2026 – men send din ansøgning hurtigst muligt, da vi løbende kalder kandidater til samtale.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About the company

We are a fast-growing, VC-backed energy-tech scale-up on a mission to connect electric vehicles directly with power markets and make smart charging completely frictionless for consumers. Our fully automated, data-driven platform forecasts energy needs, tracks electricity prices and grid conditions in real time, and optimises charging to maximise savings and unlock new revenue streams for our customers.

You will join a small, highly technical team working at the intersection of real-time data, energy markets and EV charging.




The role

As a Data Engineer, you will own and evolve the data infrastructure at the core of our optimisation platform. You’ll work closely with software engineers and optimisation specialists to ensure models and products are built on trusted, production-grade data systems.

Key responsibilities:

Design, build and operate production data pipelines and data models for time-series and event-driven workloads
Ingest and process external data feeds (market prices, asset telemetry, grid signals) into our core data platform
Improve latency, reliability and observability across the stack to support fast decision-making in live markets
Develop and maintain backtesting and simulation frameworks for new strategies and product features
Contribute to scaling the platform as the number of connected assets and markets grows




Tech stack (current and planned)

Python as the primary language
Cloud data platform (GCP or similar), containerised services and modern CI/CD
Relational databases (e.g. PostgreSQL) and time-series databases (e.g. TimescaleDB or similar)
Streaming / event-driven infrastructure and real-time data processing (e.g. Kafka, Pub/Sub, Kinesis, or equivalent)

You don’t need experience with our exact tools, but you should be comfortable working in a modern cloud/data environment and picking up new technologies.







About you

We imagine you have:

3–6 years of experience as a Data Engineer or similar, working with production-grade data systems
Strong hands-on skills in Python and SQL
Experience building and operating data pipelines for time-series, streaming or event-driven use cases
Solid understanding of reliability, performance, monitoring and testing for data-intensive systems
Experience from energy, trading, fintech, or other real-time, high-stakes domains is a strong plus
Bonus: familiarity with a systems language (e.g. Rust or C++) or with statistical / ML tooling




You’ll thrive here if you:

Enjoy end-to-end ownership of your systems, from design to production
Are pragmatic, curious, and comfortable in a startup/scale-up environment with high autonomy
Want your work to have a direct impact on decarbonisation and the future of electric mobility




What we offer

Competitive salary (50,000 - 55,000 DKK/month) plus 5–15% performance bonus
High degree of autonomy and ownership over core parts of the data platform
Close collaboration with experienced engineers and domain experts in energy and optimisation
Central Copenhagen office, flexible hybrid setup, and an informal, international team culture

Title: Department Manager
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Ralph Lauren Corporation (NYSE:RL) is a global leader in the design, marketing and distribution of premium lifestyle products in five categories: apparel, accessories, home, fragrances, and hospitality. For more than 50 years, Ralph Lauren's reputation and distinctive image have been consistently developed across an expanding number of products, brands and international markets. The Company's brand names, which include Ralph Lauren, Ralph Lauren Collection, Ralph Lauren Purple Label, Polo Ralph Lauren, Double RL, Lauren Ralph Lauren, Polo Ralph Lauren Children, Chaps, among others, constitute one of the world's most widely recognized families of consumer brands.

At Ralph Lauren, we unite and inspire the communities within our company as well as those in which we serve by amplifying voices and perspectives to create a culture of belonging, ensuring inclusion, and fairness for all. We foster a culture of inclusion through: Talent, Education & Communication, Employee Groups and Celebration.

,

Ralph Lauren is opening a new luxury store in Copenhagen this September, and we're seeking a talented Department Manager to join our leadership team!

We're looking for an experienced professional with a passion for luxury retail and a strong ability to drive sales, motivate team members, and create exceptional customer experiences. If you have the expertise to manage your department seamlessly, the eye for visual excellence, and the leadership skills to inspire your team, this is the perfect opportunity for you.

Be part of our exciting new chapter in Copenhagen and help bring the Ralph Lauren brand to life. Bring your retail knowledge, dedication to excellence, and passion for timeless style to one of the world's most iconic fashion brands.

Opening September 2026 – Apply Today!

,

STODMS1362 v1


Ensuring team members understand the vision and strategy of the store and brand, while creating a culture that ensures that there is a good rapport and partnership between all team members across all departments.
Delivering and managing company customer service initiatives and achieving results that contribute to brand performance.
Meeting customer needs while continually searching for ways to improve service, and managing the store in a way that always services the customer first and manages tasks second.
Driving sales and minimizing stock loss through audit compliance and involvement and analysing business reports to make commercial decisions/requests which drive the business.
Partnering with Store Manager to accurately forecast sales and costs and managing store budgets in line with this.
Developing the team and coaching employees.
Maintaining professional and technical knowledge of the brand and product, and educating the store team on this.
Working with the Multisite Manager to direct the execution of Polo’s promotional strategies and programs, assuring that they support Polo Ralph Lauren’s sales, marketing, and profit objectives.


,

STODMS1362 v1


Track record of leading teams and successfully managing turnover of a similar size.
Customer and people focused with in-depth knowledge of developing and implementing excellence in customer service.
Strong planning and organizational skills with the ability to multi-task and balance priorities.
Competent user of Microsoft Office applications (Outlook, Word and Excel).
Passion for the brand and an interest in fashion.
Strong business acumen and goal oriented.
Demonstrated track record of commercial success, leading and developing teams .
Excellent organizational, planning and time management skills.
Strong communication skills and able to foster positive business partnerships.
Action oriented with a proactive approach.
Passion for the brand and an interest in fashion.
Fluency in Danish and English is mandatory, both spoken and written.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
📍 Location: Baltorpvej (~20 mins from central Copenhagen)

🗣️ Language Requirement: English-speaking




Coders Connect is thrilled to be partnering with a global leader in smart pest control solutions, helping expand their Innovation Centre just outside Copenhagen.

You’ll join a startup-style unit within the company, focused on delivering next-gen IoT-powered, data-driven services.




With over 1 million IoT devices deployed globally, this team plays a critical role in transforming massive data volumes into actionable insights. We're seeking a Danish-speaking Data Engineer ready to work in a fast-paced, agile environment and build something that truly scales 🌍.




🔧 What You’ll Do

Build and maintain data pipelines that handle hourly batch data from a vast IoT device network
Work in Azure and Databricks to create scalable, high-performance cloud data infrastructure
Collaborate closely with Data Scientists, QA, Product Managers & Engineers in agile 2-week sprints
Contribute to architecture, ensuring top-notch data quality, performance, and reliability
Help transition from external consultants to an internal, capability-driven data team
Operate autonomously in a flat, startup-like structure




👨 💻 You Bring

Fluency in English (spoken & written)
Living in Denmark (No Visa support, unfortunately)
5+ Years in Data Engineering required with a slope to become Technical Lead
Proven experience with Azure Data Services and Databricks
Strong ETL pipeline skills for batch data processing and working with large data pipelines
A scale-up/startup mindset ~ proactive, self-driven, and ownership-focused
Experience in agile development environments




📍 Working Setup

Hybrid: Tuesdays onsite required, ideally 2–3 days/week
Office in Baltorpvej (approx. 20 mins from central Copenhagen)
Flat structure, high autonomy, quick decision-making




💸 What’s On Offer

Competitive Salary: DKK 65,000 - 75,000 + 10% pension
+10% employer pension contribution
5 weeks vacation + 1 extra week
Work on a meaningful, global-impact IoT product
High flexibility, real ownership, and influence from day one




❌ Please note: The company does not offer VISA sponsorship/transfer




If you're a Data Engineer looking for real-world impact at scale, in a team where your voice matters we’d love to hear from you!

📍 Working Setup

  • Hybrid: Tuesdays onsite required, ideally 2–3 days/week
  • Office in Baltorpvej (approx. 20 mins from central Copenhagen)
  • Flat structure, high autonomy, quick decision-making


💸 What’s On Offer

  • Competitive Salary: DKK 65,000 - 75,000 + 10% pension
  • +10% employer pension contribution
  • 5 weeks vacation + 1 extra week
  • Work on a meaningful, global-impact IoT product
  • High flexibility, real ownership, and influence from day one


❌ Please note: The company does not offer VISA sponsorship/transfer


If you're a Data Engineer looking for real-world impact at scale, in a team where your voice matters we’d love to hear from you!

Title: Analytics Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Spirii develops world-class charging products and digital solutions for electric vehicles. Our Digital Development department fosters a listen, learn, and lead culture, allowing us to continuously improve how we work. We're looking for a hands-on Analytics Engineer to build and shape Spirii's analytics layer and data reliability practices.

Working closely with our Data Lead and domain teams, you'll implement dbt standards, data models, tests, and documentation — turning raw data in Amazon Redshift into trusted, well-governed data products across EV charging events, subscriptions, and financial transactions. This is a unique opportunity to define best practices from day one, lay the foundations for scalable transformations and consistent metrics, and help build Spirii's data culture from the ground up.

Your Responsibilities

Data Modelling & dbt


Help build and shape our dbt project: structure, conventions, CI practices, environments, and release workflows.
Design and implement analytics-ready data models (e.g., staging/intermediate/marts, fact/dimension modelling) that enable self-serve analytics and reliable reporting.


Data Quality & Governance


Define and implement data quality practices together with domain teams: dbt tests, freshness checks, anomaly detection, and data contracts.
Improve data documentation, lineage, and discoverability.


Collaboration & Stakeholders


Partner with Engineering and Product teams to ensure analytics requirements are addressed upstream (events, schemas, identifiers, change management).
Collaborate with Finance and business teams to standardize metrics and eliminate inconsistent metric definitions across dashboards.
Contribute to a strong data culture in Spirii: patterns, templates, reviews, enablement, and data literacy.


Platform & Infrastructure


Support and evolve our ELT foundations: help improve ingestion reliability, orchestration, monitoring, and incident response (in collaboration with Engineering/Data Lead).
Optimize model performance and cost in Redshift.


About You


You care deeply about data correctness and building systems humans can trust.
You're comfortable being hands-on: you ship models, tests, and improvements yourself.
You think of data models as products with consumers — you care about who uses them, for what purpose, and whether they can be trusted over time.
You collaborate well across teams and can drive alignment on definitions and ownership.
You're excited to help define what makes a metric canonical and move away from metrics defined ad-hoc in dashboards.
You're pragmatic: you know when to implement a robust standard and when to keep it lean.


Qualifications


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
3+ years experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, or software engineering with a strong data focus.
Strong SQL skills and demonstrated experience building analytics models.
Solid dbt experience (core patterns, project structuring, tests, documentation, and deployment practices).
Experience with a modern data warehouse (Redshift preferred; BigQuery/Snowflake also relevant).
Comfortable with at least one programming language used in data work (Python preferred).
Solid understanding of ELT/ETL concepts and some hands-on experience improving pipelines, reliability, or orchestration.
Familiar with data governance concepts (access control, PII handling, GDPR-minded practices).
Experience working with BI tools and stakeholders to define consistent metrics.


Nice to Have


Experience setting up data observability tooling (or implementing monitoring patterns around dbt runs, freshness, volume, distribution changes).
Experience with event/data contract patterns in product analytics (schemas, versioning, instrumentation).
Experience with finance data and/or subscription/transaction domains.
Experience with IaC (Terraform/CDK), and/or CI/CD pipelines for data workflows.
Machine learning experience is a plus, but not required for this role.


Benefits

It is important for us to create a healthy, diverse, fun, and creative working environment, so besides all the basic benefits, we also focus a lot on social activities, flexible working hours and a good work life balance.


Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a collaborative, inclusive, and open workplace designed to fit your needs and support work-life balance.
Competitive Package: A competitive salary, an 11% company pension plan, and a comprehensive benefits package.
Time to Recharge: Benefit from 32 paid vacation days (5 weeks + 5 extra) and paid parental leave to focus on what matters most.
Career Growth: Access professional development opportunities and grow your career in a high-impact, high-growth industry.
Stay Energized: Enjoy excellent coffee, tea, free snacks, and subsidized lunches at the office.
Stay Active: Use our Spirii gym and shared company car to keep moving.
Make an Impact: Be part of a team driving the future of sustainable energy while working on meaningful projects.
Two innovation days per month for YOU to choose what you work on.


The position is a permanent position in Copenhagen, Denmark. Danish language is not a requirement, but Fluent English language skills are required.

About Spirii – Who We Are

Spirii is a full-service e-Mobility provider, which offers hardware and software charging solutions to companies in the private and the public sector. Spirii has recently celebrated its six-year anniversary as a founded company, however the people in Spirii have extensive experience working with electric cars and charging, as well as development of future transport- and energy systems.

Spirii is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences. Spirii strives to create an inclusive and diverse workplace, and we encourage all qualified applicants to apply regardless of age, gender, race, sexual/religious orientation, or disability

Title: Consultant for Data Science Consultancy Team
Place: Copenhagen Metropolitan Area
Description:
Consultant for Data Science Consultancy Team

Are you passionate about applying Data Science in real-world scenarios? Do you want to leverage mathematics, statistics, and programming to help our clients make better decisions? Are you driven by data and analysis, and eager to translate insights into actionable recommendations? Join Annalect’s Data Science Consultancy team!




About the Role

We are looking for a new colleague to join Annalect, Omnicom Media Group’s specialist unit for data-driven analytics and decision support systems. Our work includes analyses that quantify the impact of our clients’ marketing activities and provide the foundation for ongoing optimization. Annalect consists of 60 employees, with 20 dedicated to the Data Science Consultancy team. Our team utilizes mathematics, statistics, and econometrics to process large volumes of data, delivering business-oriented decision support. As a Data Science Consultant, you will be involved in the entire project lifecycle for our clients – from data collection and coding, to building and optimizing statistical models in R, and communicating results and recommendations to clients. You will work closely with a project lead and the team, receiving ongoing support and feedback. As you gain experience, you will have the opportunity to conduct analyses independently, manage client communications, and deliver your own projects.

Key Responsibilities:

Collect client data and extract data from media systems
Programming and data processing
Structure, code, and interpret data
Develop statistical models in R
Analyze results and prepare recommendations in close collaboration with a project lead
Prepare deliverables for clients in PowerPoint and dashboards
Maintain ongoing dialogue with clients and media agencies throughout the project
Lead your own projects




Your Profile

We imagine that you:

Hold a relevant academic degree (e.g., Cand.Merc. (MAT), Cand.polit. IMM, AEF, or similar)
Have basic experience with statistical models and methodologies
Possess strong analytical skills
Are proficient in R Studio (or other programming languages such as Python, SAS, VBA, SQL and are eager to further develop your skills in R Studio)
Are experienced in Excel and PowerPoint
Are interested in building an understanding of marketing, digital platforms, and the media landscape




What We Offer

We offer a position in an exciting company with many opportunities. You will be part of a strong professional community and work alongside some of the industry’s most talented specialists. We also have a vibrant social environment, including Friday bars, annual parties, company football team, DHL race, and more. Our culture is relaxed, with room for fun and focus for deep work on projects.




Interested?

We look forward to receiving your application and CV as soon as possible. We conduct interviews on an ongoing basis and reserve the right to close the posting once the right candidate has been found.

If you have any questions about the position or the company, please contact Analytics Director Anders Mousten at anders.mousten@omc.com.

Title: Student Assistant
Place: Kongens Lyngby, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Student Assistant – Portfolio Data Analyst

Are you passionate about data analytics and looking to apply your skills in a real business setting? Do you enjoy working with data and turning it into clear insights? Then you might be our new Student Assistant in Hempel’s Innovation Portfolio Management team!

What can you look forward to in this role?

We work with innovation and new product development, ensuring structure, transparency, and strong decision-making across our portfolio. As a Student Assistant, you will support how we work with data and help transform it into insights used for decision-making.

As part of the team, you will support data management and analysis activities across projects. You will work closely with the team to ensure data quality, build reporting, and contribute to improving how we use data in Portfolio Management.

What will your responsibilities be?


Support Portfolio Management reporting and analysis
Assist with maintaining and improving data quality
Help structure and clean data across different sources
Assist in improving processes and ways of working within Portfolio Management
Ad hoc analysis and support


What do we look for in you?


You are currently studying a relevant degree
Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail
Good skills in Excel (experience with Power BI is a plus)
Interest in working with data and turning it into insights
Structured and able to manage multiple tasks


What do we offer you?

You will join a collaborative and supportive team where you will gain hands-on experience with data analytics. We offer a flexible working environment with an office based in Kgs. Lyngby.

Ready to join us?

Come and be a valued part of a close-knit team based in Kgs. Lyngby, working around 20 hours per week with flexibility to match your academic schedule.

To apply for this position, please submit your application letter with a CV in English through our recruitment system by clicking the "Apply" link at the top. For questions regarding the role, email peham@hempel.com

Please note: we do not accept applications via email due to GDPR. If your email contains personal information, it will be disregarded.

We value diversity and encourage applications from all qualified individuals. Applications will be treated with respect and you will be contacted if we see a potential match.

Application due

2026-04-30

Seniority Level

Internship

Job Functions

Administrative, Project Management

Industry

Chemicals

At Hempel, you’re welcomed to a global community of +7000 colleagues around the world. A workplace built on shared trust, mutual respect and support for each other.

We’re on a mission to double our impact. To succeed, we need bright minds and team players. Which is why, from day one, your input, ideas and initiative are not only welcomed, but expected. In return, you will enjoy great opportunities for development in a growing global company – and be part of the solution by contributing to our global impact.

At Hempel, we are committed to everyone feeling safe, valued and treated equally, in an environment where each employee can bring their authentic selves to work.  We believe that inclusion is key to innovation and by bringing together the most diverse perspectives and talents, we can achieve great things, together.

Sørge for at butikken altid fremstår ren og salgsklar

Opfyldning og trimning af de forskellige afdelinger
Give den bedste service til vores kunder

Du arbejder passioneret for at give vores kunder den bedste kundeoplevelse
Du er smilende, positiv og kan holde hovedet koldt i en travl hverdag

Title: Operations Associate
Place: Copenhagen Metropolitan Area
Description:
Company Description

Alrik builds AI solutions for the construction industry logistics supply chain. Alrik empowers its customers to make data-driven decisions, improve efficiency, enhance sustainability, and boost profitability. 




Role Description

This is a part-time role for an Operations Associate based in Copenhagen. You will work closely with our customers in their daily transport operations — and support the Alrik team with operational efficiency improvements, data reports, and business intelligence.




The role combines hands-on operational execution with analytical thinking. Strong written and verbal skills, online and in-person

Students and alumni from leading technical universities or business schools are encouraged to apply and can combine the role with their studies
Proficiency in English and Danish is required. Swedish is a plus
Proven curiosity and ability in SQL and/or AI-tools

Title: Ungarbejder - 7 timer - Dragør
Place: Dragør, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Introduktion

Er du under 18 år? Og drømmer du om at få et job, der giver lidt ekstra penge til dig selv hver måned? Så er du måske vores nye ungarbejder!

På #teamlidl bliver du en del af et seriøst godt sammenhold. Og som ungarbejder i Lidl får du et Lidl-tillæg på 5 kr. mere i timen.*

Mød din nye kollega Katrine

”Jeg er så glad for at arbejde i Lidl! Her er plads til alle, og alle bliver hørt. Vi har et rigtig godt team, hvor alle er gode til at hjælpe hinanden. Selve arbejdsmiljøet består af gode kunder, søde kollegaer og en flot butik,” fortæller Katrine, der er 17 år gammel og ungarbejder i Lidl.

Kan du bidrage til godt humør, fællesskab og en god service? Så er det måske dig, vi står og mangler!

På #teamlidl kommer du på hold med verdensklasse kollegaer. Når du starter, klæder vi dig på gennem et grundigt oplæringsforløb i vores koncepter. Vi går nemlig op i, at du lærer tingene rigtigt fra start, at du lykkes i dit job, og at du bidrager til et stærkt sammenhold med dine kollegaer.

Grænseløse karrieremulighederSom ungarbejder er der mange forskellige udviklingsmuligheder hos os. Har du f.eks. ambitioner om at gøre karriere i dagligvarebranchen? Så er jobbet som ungarbejder det perfekte sted at starte. Din erfaring som ungarbejder vil nemlig kun være en fordel, hvis du senere ønsker at søge ind på vores elevuddannelse, som vi mener, er Danmarks suverænt bedste! Det er ligeledes en stor fordel at have butikserfaring, hvis du drømmer om at arbejde på vores hovedkontor.


Ift. den overenskomstmæssig takst pr. marts 2025.


Om jobbet

Som ungarbejder hos os skal du, først og fremmest, hjælpe dine kollegaer, så vores kunder altid får en god indkøbsoplevelse i butikken. Du ved, at du har gjort dit arbejde godt, når du har ydet en god service – og det gør dig glad! Dine opgaver er blandt andet at:


Give vores kunder en god oplevelse i butikken sammen med dine kollegaer
Få butikken til at shine, ved at fylde op og trimme hylderne så de altid står snorlige

Introduktion

Er du under 18 år? Og drømmer du om at få et job, der giver lidt ekstra penge til dig selv hver måned? Så er du måske vores nye ungarbejder!

På #teamlidl bliver du en del af et seriøst godt sammenhold. Og som ungarbejder i Lidl får du et Lidl-tillæg på 5 kr. mere i timen.*

Mød din nye kollega Katrine

”Jeg er så glad for at arbejde i Lidl! Her er plads til alle, og alle bliver hørt. Vi har et rigtig godt team, hvor alle er gode til at hjælpe hinanden. Selve arbejdsmiljøet består af gode kunder, søde kollegaer og en flot butik,” fortæller Katrine, der er 17 år gammel og ungarbejder i Lidl.

Kan du bidrage til godt humør, fællesskab og en god service? Så er det måske dig, vi står og mangler!

På #teamlidl kommer du på hold med verdensklasse kollegaer. Når du starter, klæder vi dig på gennem et grundigt oplæringsforløb i vores koncepter. Vi går nemlig op i, at du lærer tingene rigtigt fra start, at du lykkes i dit job, og at du bidrager til et stærkt sammenhold med dine kollegaer.

Title: Full-time Analyst for SEB Corporate Finance
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
SEB Corporate Finance is looking for talented individuals who will play a central role in our M&A and ECM advisory service activities and get a unique opportunity to develop a professional career in investment banking.

With a long and proven track record, we are recognized as a leading Nordic investment bank, offering clients a single point of entry to the region with respect to M&A, IPOs, equity issues and general financial advisory services. Our clients are mainly large Nordic corporates, institutional investors, international companies with M&A activities in Northern Europe and private equity firms.

SEB Corporate Finance is part of SEB Investment Banking’s team consisting of c. 400 professionals located in e.g., the Nordic capitals, Frankfurt, London, Warsaw and New York. You will become part of a dynamic and international environment with highly motivated and experienced colleagues.

About The Job

At SEB Corporate Finance, you will experience how advisory services are handled at the highest professional level. Your tasks will include:


Information gathering and processing for company, industry and market analyses
Corporate valuations and financial analyses
Preparation of company presentations, board presentations, investor materials, etc.
Participation in the execution of live M&A and ECM projects
Interaction with other SEB teams as well as external stakeholders


The role is based in Copenhagen, but frequent interactions with international colleagues during e.g., cross-border transactions should be expected.

Who are you?

As part of our entrepreneurial mindset, we cherish ambition. In addition to exceptional job skills, we are looking for people who share our values, who are guided by a strong belief that entrepreneurial minds and innovative companies are key in creating a better world and people who are motivated and care for ambition.

The candidate has zero to three years full-time experience from a similar position. We expect that the candidate has completed a master’s degree within economics or finance (e.g., cand.polit. or cand.merc.) at the top 10% of their class. The candidate must be fluent in Danish and English, both written and orally. Further, we expect you to:


Be ambitious, dedicated and outgoing with a genuine interest in corporate finance
Have a strong level of numeracy and analytical, as well as communicative, skills
Be willing to learn and constantly develop your skill set
Work in a methodical and structured way with a focus on quality, details and deadlines


Experience from a similar role in investment banking, management consulting, private equity or corporate positions with exposure to corporate finance related tasks is strongly preferred.

What We Offer

SEB Corporate Finance offers an attractive and international learning environment. We aim to attract an exceptional individual with a desire to pursue a career in investment banking. You will work side by side with experienced colleagues and get the chance not only to quickly learn the business, but also to build up your network. In addition, you will be enrolled in SEB Investment Banking’s structured training program.

It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity are crucial for our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe and where they come from.

Learn more about working at SEB on www.sebgroup.com/career

Application details

Attach your CV, your current grade transcript and a personal letter describing yourself and how you can contribute to the corporate finance team. Interviews will commence on an ongoing basis at SEB’s Danish offices located at Bernstorffsgade 50, 1577 Copenhagen V, so please feel free to send your application today and no later than 6 May 2026.

If you have any questions about the position, please contact:

Nina Ravn, Associate, at +45 3135 7859 or Kristoffer Visbjerg, Vice President, at +45 6166 0693.

Title: Administrativ kontorassistent
Place: Holbæk Municipality, Region Zealand, Denmark
Description:
🚧 Administrativ kontorassistent søges til Nordens Asfalt 🚧

Nordens Asfalt søger en administrativ kontorassistent til en central rolle i vores organisation med base i Holbæk, hvorfra arbejdet vil foregå.

Stillingen indebærer en bred vifte af administrative og koordinerende opgaver med tæt samarbejde med entrepriseleder samt den daglige drift.

Arbejdsopgaver:
• Administrativ understøttelse af entrepriseledelsen
• Planlægning og koordinering af projekter og ressourcer
• Opfølgning på drift og bemanding
• Bidrag til struktur og effektiv afvikling af opgaver i en travl hverdag

Kvalifikationer:
• Erfaring fra en tilsvarende administrativ stilling, gerne inden for entreprenør-, bygge- eller asfaltbranchen
• Gode kompetencer inden for planlægning og koordinering
• Evne til at bevare overblik i perioder med højt aktivitetsniveau
• Samarbejdsorienteret tilgang og forståelse for driftsmæssige processer
• Erfaring med at understøtte et velfungerende arbejdsmiljø gennem tydelig kommunikation og hensigtsmæssig opgaveprioritering

Vi tilbyder en alsidig stilling i en virksomhed med høj aktivitet og korte beslutningsveje.

Hos os er erfaring en styrke, og vi værdsætter solide kompetencer – uanset hvor i arbejdslivet du befinder dig. Det er derfor ikke afgørende, om du har mange år tilbage på arbejdsmarkedet, så længe du bidrager med engagement og faglighed.

Ansøgning:
Interesserede kandidater bedes fremsende CV samt en kort ansøgning til jbt@nordensasfalt.dk.
Husk at oplyse hvilket område du bor i samt dine kontaktoplysninger.

Vi behandler ansøgninger løbende.

Title: Data Entry Associate
Place: Gladsaxe, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
TFS HealthScience is a leading global mid-size Contract Research Organization (CRO) that partners with biotechnology and pharmaceutical companies throughout their entire clinical development journey. Our expertise includes full-service capabilities, resourcing, and Functional Service (FSP) solutions.

As part of our SRS/FSP team, you will be dedicated to one sponsor, a global pharmaceutical or biotechnology company that is an industry leader within its therapeutic area.

Key Responsibilities


Data entry into eCRF (electronic Case Report Form).
Query resolution support.
Document management support.
Monitoring visit preparation.
Audit readiness support.


Qualifications


EDC / eCRF system experience (Medidata Rave, Viedoc, Oracle InForm).
GCP awareness — full certification preferred.
Attention to detail and query resolution experience.
Clinical trial or healthcare data background preferred.
Local language mandatory.
Comfortable working fractional (0.25 FTE) across multiple sites.


What We Offer

We provide opportunities for personal and professional growth in a rewarding environment. You will join a team that values collaboration, quality, and making a difference in the lives of patients.

Our core values of Trust, Quality, Passion, Flexibility, and Sustainability guide our decisions and shape our culture. By aligning on these values, we foster collaboration, innovation, and a shared commitment to excellence. Together, we make a difference.

A Bit More About Us

Our journey began over 30 years ago in Sweden, in the city of Lund. As a full-service global CRO, we build solution-driven teams working toward a healthier future. Bringing together over 600 professionals, TFS delivers tailored clinical research services in more than 50 countries, offering flexible clinical development and strategic resourcing solutions across key therapeutic areas including Dermatology, Neuroscience, Oncology, and Ophthalmology.

TFS HealthScience is a global, mid-sized contract research organization (CRO) partnering with biotechnology and pharmaceutical companies across the full clinical development lifecycle. Our expertise spans full-service clinical development, project-based support, strategic resourcing, and Functional Service Provider (FSP) solutions, matching experienced professionals to roles where they deliver the greatest impact.
